"Sigh, I was hoping to get the Flower Wine Monk's Liquor worm and refine it as my vital Gu. But now, the Flower Wine Monk's corpse is nowhere to be found, and I don't know when I can find it, or if someone else finds it. To be safe, I'll choose a Moonlight Gu. " Fang Yuan sighed in his heart as he walked towards the left wall. On one of the levels slightly above the hole in the wall, there was a row of silver plates. Each plate had a Gu worm on it. This Gu worm was crystal clear and curved like a moon. It was like a piece of blue crystal, and under the contrast of the silver chassis, it gave off a serene aura. The Gu's name was Moonlight, and it was the Guyue clan's guardian Gu. Most of the clansmen chose it as their vital Gu. It was not a natural Gu worm, but it was cultivated by the secret method of the Guyue clan.

It could not be found anywhere else, and it could be said to be the symbol of the Guyue clan. They were both Rank one Moonlight Gu, but the difference was minute. Fang Yuan randomly picked one and held it in his hand. The Moonlight Gu was very light, as light as a thin piece of paper. It only occupied the center of his palm and was about the size of an ordinary jade pendant. Fang Yuan held it in his hand. Through it, he could see the lines on his palm that had been concealed. After taking one last look and finding that there were no problems, Fang Yuan put it in his pocket and left the Gu room. There was a long queue outside the Gu room, and when a young man saw Fang Yuan walking out, he quickly ran into the Gu room excitedly. If it was someone else, their first reaction would be to take the Gu worm home and quickly refine it. But Fang Yuan was not in a hurry to do so. He was still thinking about the Liquor worm. The Liquor worm was even more precious. Although the Moonlight Gu was a specialty of the Guyue village, it was not as helpful to the Gu Master as the Liquor worm. After leaving the Gu room, Fang Yuan went straight to the wine shop. "Shopkeeper, give me two jars of aged wine." Fang Yuan dug into his pocket and took out the remaining Yuan stone fragments, placing them on the counter. These days, he would come here to buy wine, and then wander around the village to search, intending to attract the Liquor worm. The shopkeeper was a short, middle-aged fat man with an oily face. After these days, he had already remembered Fang Yuan. "Customer, you're here." As he greeted, he stretched out his thick and short fat hand and skillfully wiped away Fang Yuan's Yuan stone fragments. He then weighed it in his hand, and felt that the weight was not bad.
S-REV-009.summary: After the academy lecture, Fang Yuan enters the Gu Room, a space filled with various Rank one Gu worms. He observes the different Gu housed in various containers and reflects on the significance of the vital Gu, which is tied to a Gu Master's life. Still secretly hoping to find the Liquor worm and the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance, he reluctantly decides to select a Moonlight Gu as a temporary measure, noting its commonality within the Guyue clan and its lesser value compared to the Liquor worm.

continuityMutations.content: His dwindling supply of Yuan stone from repeatedly buying wine to attract the Liquor worm.
Fang Yuan's routine of buying two jars of wine every day for a week.
Recognizes Fang Yuan as a regular customer for wine purchases.
A place for commerce and social interaction within the village.

C-REV-01.transition: Exited the wine shop, carrying two jars of wine.

S-REV-010.prose:
It could not be found anywhere else, and it could be said to be the symbol of the Guyue clan. They were both Rank one Moonlight Gu, but the difference was minute. Fang Yuan randomly picked one and held it in his hand. The Moonlight Gu was very light, as light as a thin piece of paper. It only occupied the center of his palm and was about the size of an ordinary jade pendant. Fang Yuan held it in his hand. Through it, he could see the lines on his palm that had been concealed. After taking one last look and finding that there were no problems, Fang Yuan put it in his pocket and left the Gu room. There was a long queue outside the Gu room, and when a young man saw Fang Yuan walking out, he quickly ran into the Gu room excitedly. If it was someone else, their first reaction would be to take the Gu worm home and quickly refine it. But Fang Yuan was not in a hurry to do so. He was still thinking about the Liquor worm. The Liquor worm was even more precious. Although the Moonlight Gu was a specialty of the Guyue village, it was not as helpful to the Gu Master as the Liquor worm. After leaving the Gu room, Fang Yuan went straight to the wine shop. "Shopkeeper, give me two jars of aged wine." Fang Yuan dug into his pocket and took out the remaining Yuan stone fragments, placing them on the counter. These days, he would come here to buy wine, and then wander around the village to search, intending to attract the Liquor worm. The shopkeeper was a short, middle-aged fat man with an oily face. After these days, he had already remembered Fang Yuan. "Customer, you're here." As he greeted, he stretched out his thick and short fat hand and skillfully wiped away Fang Yuan's Yuan stone fragments. He then weighed it in his hand, and felt that the weight was not bad.

The smile on the shopkeeper's face became more amiable. Yuan stone was this world's currency, used to measure the value of all goods. At the same time, it was a condensation of the essence of heaven and earth, and could be used by itself to help the Gu Master cultivate better. It had both monetary and commodity attributes, very similar to gold on Earth. Earth had a single gold standard, and this world had a single Yuan stone standard. Compared to gold, Yuan stone's purchasing power was quite shocking. However, no matter how much Yuan stone there was, it could not support Fang Yuan's continuous consumption. "Two jars of wine a day, it's been a whole seven days. The Yuan stone that I saved up is almost all gone. "Carrying the two jars of wine out of the wine shop, Fang Yuan frowned slightly. Once you become a Gu Master, you can directly extract pure primeval essence from the Yuan stone to replenish the primeval sea in the aperture. Thus, to Gu Masters, Yuan stone was not only a currency, but also a cultivation aid. With enough Yuan stone, the cultivation speed could increase by a lot, and this could more or less make up for some of the shortcomings in aptitude. "Tomorrow I won't have any Yuan stone to buy wine, but the Liquor worm still hasn't shown up. Do I really have to refine the Moonlight Gu into a lifebound Gu? "Fang Yuan was not resigned. Walking out of the tavern, Fang Yuan held two jars of wine in his hands, thinking as he walked, "The academy elder said that the first person to refine a lifebound Gu in this examination will get twenty Yuan stone as a reward. Right now, I'm afraid many people are working hard at home to refine Gu worms and fight for first place. Unfortunately, refining a lifebound Gu is a great test of aptitude. Those with good aptitude have great advantages. With my C grade aptitude, without any other tricks, I have no hope of winning. " At this moment, Guyue Fangzheng's voice came from behind, "Brother, you really came to the tavern to get drunk again! Follow me, Uncle and Aunt want to see you.
S-REV-010.summary: After leaving the Gu Room, Fang Yuan goes directly to the Wine Shop and buys two jars of aged wine, using his remaining Yuan stone fragments. The Shopkeeper, recognizing Fang Yuan, amiably completes the transaction. Fang Yuan reflects on his depleting funds and the lack of success in attracting the Liquor worm, making him reconsider refining the Moonlight Gu as his vital Gu, which would mean giving up the reward for being first to refine.

continuityMutations.content: The realization that his brother Fang Yuan, who once suppressed him, is now in a 'depressed state,' which brings him an inexplicable sense of joy.
A new sense of resolve to no longer defer to Fang Yuan, despite his lingering discomfort.
Guyue Fangzheng's direct confrontation about his drinking and behavior, and his request to return home.

relationshipMutations.type: Shifting power dynamic with growing resentment and newfound courage.

S-REV-011.prose:
The smile on the shopkeeper's face became more amiable. Yuan stone was this world's currency, used to measure the value of all goods. At the same time, it was a condensation of the essence of heaven and earth, and could be used by itself to help the Gu Master cultivate better. It had both monetary and commodity attributes, very similar to gold on Earth. Earth had a single gold standard, and this world had a single Yuan stone standard. Compared to gold, Yuan stone's purchasing power was quite shocking. However, no matter how much Yuan stone there was, it could not support Fang Yuan's continuous consumption. "Two jars of wine a day, it's been a whole seven days. The Yuan stone that I saved up is almost all gone. "Carrying the two jars of wine out of the wine shop, Fang Yuan frowned slightly. Once you become a Gu Master, you can directly extract pure primeval essence from the Yuan stone to replenish the primeval sea in the aperture. Thus, to Gu Masters, Yuan stone was not only a currency, but also a cultivation aid. With enough Yuan stone, the cultivation speed could increase by a lot, and this could more or less make up for some of the shortcomings in aptitude. "Tomorrow I won't have any Yuan stone to buy wine, but the Liquor worm still hasn't shown up. Do I really have to refine the Moonlight Gu into a lifebound Gu? "Fang Yuan was not resigned. Walking out of the tavern, Fang Yuan held two jars of wine in his hands, thinking as he walked, "The academy elder said that the first person to refine a lifebound Gu in this examination will get twenty Yuan stone as a reward. Right now, I'm afraid many people are working hard at home to refine Gu worms and fight for first place. Unfortunately, refining a lifebound Gu is a great test of aptitude. Those with good aptitude have great advantages. With my C grade aptitude, without any other tricks, I have no hope of winning. " At this moment, Guyue Fangzheng's voice came from behind, "Brother, you really came to the tavern to get drunk again! Follow me, Uncle and Aunt want to see you.

" Fang Yuan stopped and turned around. He found that his brother was no longer talking with his head lowered like before. The two brothers' eyes met. A gust of wind blew, blowing the elder brother's messy black hair and blowing the younger brother's clothes. In the short span of a month, everything had changed. The Awakening Ceremony a week ago was a huge change for both the elder brother and the younger brother. The elder brother, Fang Yuan, fell from the clouds, the halo of a genius ruthlessly taken away. The younger brother, on the other hand, began to shine, like a rising star. This change, for the younger brother, Guyue Fangzheng, was like the world had turned upside down. He finally tasted what his brother had felt in the past, being entrusted with hope by others, being looked at with envy and jealousy. He felt as if he had suddenly moved from a dark corner to a heaven filled with light. Every day when he woke up, he felt as if he was in a beautiful dream. The difference in treatment was like heaven and earth, and he still found it hard to believe. At the same time, there was a strong sense of discomfort. Discomfort. All of a sudden, he went from being unknown to being closely watched and pointed at. Sometimes, when Fangzheng was walking on the road, he would hear people talking about him and praising him. He would feel his face heat up and be at a loss of what to do. He would avoid eye contact and almost forget where he was going! In the first ten days, Guyue Fangzheng inexplicably lost a lot of weight, but he was more energetic. From the deepest part of his heart, something called "confidence" began to grow. "This is how big brother used to feel. It was wonderful and painful!"

He could not help but think of his brother, Guyue Fang Yuan. Facing such discussions and attention, how did his brother deal with them in the past? He subconsciously began to imitate Fang Yuan, pretending to be expressionless, but soon found that he was not cut out for it. Sometimes in school, a girl's shout would make him blush. On the road, the teasing of the aunties made him flee many times. He was like a baby learning how to walk, stumbling to adapt to his new life. In the process, he could not help but hear rumors about his brother. He was depressed, became a drunkard, did not return home at night, and slept in school. At first, he was very shocked. His brother, such a powerful and talented existence, had actually become like this?! But slowly, he began to understand. His brother was also a normal person. After experiencing such a setback and blow, it was inevitable that he would be depressed. Along with this understanding, Fangzheng faintly felt an indescribable sense of joy. This feeling of joy was something he did not want to admit, but it did exist. His brother, who was praised as a genius, who used to suppress him like a shadow, was now in such a depressed state. From another perspective, this was proof of his own growth, wasn't it? He was outstanding, this was the truth! Therefore, seeing Fang Yuan carrying the wine jar, his hair in a mess, and his clothes in disarray, Guyue Fangzheng heaved a sigh of relief in his heart. His breathing inexplicably became much more relaxed. But he said, "Brother, you can't drink anymore. You can't go on like this. You don't know how worried those who care about you are.

You have to pull yourself together! " Fang Yuan was expressionless and did not speak. The two brothers looked at each other. Younger brother Guyue Fangzheng's eyes were shining, revealing a sharp look. Older brother Fang Yuan's eyes, however, were dark and deep, like an ancient lake. Such a pair of eyes made Fangzheng feel an inexplicable pressure. After staring at each other for a short while, he subconsciously shifted his gaze to the other side. But when he realized it, a wave of anger rose in his heart. A wave of anger towards himself. What's wrong with me? I don't even have the courage to look at my brother in the eye? I've changed, I've completely changed! As he thought of this, his gaze sharpened and swept over again. But Fang Yuan was no longer looking at him. Instead, he carried a jar of wine in one hand and walked past him. His calm voice was heard, "What are you waiting for? Let's go." Fangzheng's breathing became erratic. He no longer had a place to vent the anger that had accumulated in his heart. This made him feel an indescribable depression. Seeing that his brother had walked far away, he could only hurry up and follow. But this time, his head was no longer lowered. Instead, it was raised to face the setting sun.

His gaze was fixated on his own feet, stepping on his brother Fang Yuan's shadow step by step.
S-REV-011.summary: Walking through the village, Fang Yuan is approached by his brother, Guyue Fangzheng, who, now emboldened by his A-grade talent, confronts Fang Yuan about his drinking and apparent depression. Fangzheng reflects on his newfound confidence and the complex emotions of joy and guilt he feels regarding his brother's downfall. Fang Yuan, however, remains expressionless, and his ancient eyes make Fangzheng uncomfortable. After a brief staring contest, Fang Yuan walks away, leaving Fangzheng to follow with a mixture of anger and a new resolve, walking in his brother's shadow.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan realizes his uncle and aunt are plotting to disinherit and frame him for his parents' inheritance, based on their adoption offer and past life knowledge.
Uncle Guyue reveals his plan to frame Fang Yuan to prevent him from inheriting his parents' property, due to Fang Yuan's rejection of adoption.
Aunt Guyue shows her complicity in the scheme to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him, expressing anxiety about the inheritance.
Fangzheng is unaware of his uncle and aunt's true intentions, believing their adoption offer to be genuine kindness.
Fang Yuan gains knowledge about the Guyue family's inheritance rules concerning the eldest son at sixteen years old.
Fang Yuan understands his uncle and aunt's true intentions behind the adoption offer and later their plot to frame him.
Fang Yuan plans to secure his parents' inheritance and the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance, recognizing the challenges of his C-grade aptitude and limited Yuan stones.
Fang Yuan recalls how his uncle and aunt disinherited him in a previous life and is determined to avoid the same fate.
Fang Yuan is a Rank one C-grade Gu Master, attempting to refine a Moonlight Gu.
Uncle Guyue orchestrates a plan to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him, aiming to secure his parents' inheritance.
Uncle Guyue pretends to be benevolent towards Fangzheng to win him over.
Uncle Guyue is aware of the Guyue family's inheritance rules and uses them to his advantage.
Aunt Guyue is complicit in her husband's plan to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him.
Aunt Guyue plays the role of a loving guardian to Fangzheng to gain his loyalty.
Fangzheng is easily swayed by his uncle and aunt's gestures of kindness and gratefully accepts their adoption offer and resources.
Fangzheng is an A-grade talent Gu Master, dedicated to cultivation and eager to prove himself to his adopted parents.
Shen Mama is the most valued servant of Aunt Guyue and manages the household.

C-REV-01.transition: walked out of the hall, carrying a wine jar

C-REV-04.transition: was led into a room by a servant

S-REV-012.prose:
Chapter 9
The red sun was setting in the west, but it had not completely set.
There was still light in the sky, but everything seemed to be covered in a layer of grey.
Looking through the window, he could see the mountains in the distance gradually closing in on the heavy darkness.
The living room was dim, and Uncle and Aunt were sitting on the main seats. Their expressions were shrouded in a layer of shadow, and could not be seen clearly.
Seeing the two jars of wine that Fang Yuan had brought along, Uncle Guyue's brows furrowed into a knot. He said, "Time flies, and you are already fifteen years old.
Both of you have Gu Master aptitude, especially Fangzheng. Uncle and Aunt are very proud of you.
I will give each of you six Yuan stones, take them.
Refining Gu consumes a lot of primeval essence, so you will need these Yuan stones. "
Saying so, a servant came over and gave Fang Yuan and Fangzheng each a small bag.
Fang Yuan kept the bag, remaining silent.
Fangzheng immediately opened the bag, and saw six oval-shaped white Yuan stones inside.
Immediately, his face was filled with gratitude, and he stood up from his seat, bowing to Uncle and Aunt, "Thank you Uncle and Aunt, I need Yuan stones to replenish my primeval essence.
You've raised me up, and I'll remember your kindness in my heart, and I'll never forget it! "
Uncle smiled and nodded.
Aunt quickly waved her hand, and said to Fangzheng warmly, "Sit down, sit down.
Although you two are not our biological children, we have always raised you as our own.
We are also proud of you for having such a promising future.
Sigh, we do not have any children, and sometimes we wish that you two could really become our children, that would be great. "
There was a deep meaning behind these words. Fangzheng did not understand, but Fang Yuan frowned slightly.
As expected, Uncle continued, "I have discussed with your Aunt, and we want to let you two be adopted into our family, and become a real family.
Fangzheng, I wonder if you are willing? "
Fangzheng was stunned for a moment, but his face quickly lit up with joy, and he readily agreed, "To be honest, ever since my parents passed away, I've been longing for a family reunion.
It's great to be able to become a family with Uncle and Aunt! "
His aunt's expression relaxed and she smiled. "Then you are our good son. Are you still going to call us uncle and aunt?"
"Father, Mother." Fangzheng came to a sudden realization and quickly corrected himself.
Uncle and Aunt both laughed.
"Good son, it's not in vain that we've raised you since you were five years old. We've raised you for ten whole years." His aunt wiped away her tears.
His uncle looked at the silent Fang Yuan and said gently, "Fang Yuan, what do you think?"
Fang Yuan shook his head in silence.
"Brother." Guyue Fangzheng wanted to persuade him, but was stopped by his uncle.
His uncle's tone didn't change as he continued, "Since that's the case, nephew Fang Yuan, we won't force you.
But you're already fifteen years old, it's time for you to start a family. This way, it'll be easier for you to inherit the Fang Family's bloodline.
I've prepared two hundred pieces of Yuan stone for you, consider it your financial support. "
"Two hundred pieces of Yuan stone!"
Fangzheng's eyes widened. He had never seen so many pieces of Yuan stone before, and he couldn't help but reveal an envious expression.
Who knew that Fang Yuan would still shake his head.
Fangzheng was puzzled, but his uncle's expression changed slightly, and his aunt's face darkened.
"Uncle, Aunt, if there's nothing else, I'll take my leave."
Fang Yuan didn't give them a chance to speak. After saying that, he picked up the wine jar and walked out of the hall.
Fangzheng stood up. "Father, Mother, Brother can't figure it out right now. Why don't you let me persuade him?"
His uncle waved his hand and let out a long sigh. "Sigh, this can't be forced. I'm already very happy that you have the heart.
Someone, take Young Master Fangzheng away and make sure he stays well. "
"Then I'll take my leave." After Fangzheng left, the hall fell into silence.
The sun had completely set, and the hall became even darker.
After a while, his uncle's cold voice came from the darkness. "It seems that little bastard Fang Yuan has seen through our plan."
In the Guyue family's rules, it was stated that the eldest son at the age of sixteen had the right to inherit the family's property.
Fang Yuan's parents had already passed away, leaving behind a sizable inheritance that was' safeguarded 'by his aunt and uncle.
The value of this inheritance was not something that a mere two hundred pieces of Yuan stone could compare to.
If Fang Yuan were to be adopted by his aunt and uncle like Fangzheng, he wouldn't have the right to inherit this inheritance.
If Fang Yuan left the family at the age of fifteen, he wouldn't be in line with the family's inheritance rules.
"Fortunately, we managed to win over Fangzheng, and Fang Yuan only has a C grade aptitude." His uncle sighed again, feeling extremely fortunate.
"Then, Master, Fang Yuan clearly wants to leave the family at sixteen. What should we do?" His aunt's tone became anxious when she thought of the inheritance.
"Hmph, since he has bad intentions, then he can't blame us.
As long as we catch him before he leaves the family, we can chase him out of the family and deprive him of the right to inherit the inheritance. "His uncle snorted coldly.
"But Fang Yuan, this little bastard, is very smart. How could he make a mistake?" His aunt was puzzled.
His uncle immediately rolled his eyes and scolded in a low voice, "You're really stupid!
If he doesn't make a mistake, then why can't we frame him?
Let that girl, Shen Cui, seduce Fang Yuan first, then cry out that she's molesting him. We'll catch him red-handed on the spot, then frame him for drunkenness and madness. Do you think we can't chase Fang Yuan out? "
"Master, you still have a way. What a brilliant plan!" His aunt was immediately overjoyed.
The dense darkness of the night covered the sky, and the stars were mostly covered by the drifting dark clouds.
Every household in the village gradually lit up their lights.
Guyue Fangzheng was led into a room.
"Young Master Fangzheng, this is a room that Master personally instructed Old Slave to specially prepare for you." Shen Mama eagerly introduced. She bowed, her face filled with a flattering smile.
Fangzheng's eyes lit up as he looked around.
This room was twice the size of his previous one. There was a spacious bed in the center, a sandalwood desk by the window sill, and an exquisite writing brush, ink, paper, and inkstone. The surrounding walls were decorated with exquisite decorations.
Even the floor under his feet wasn't ordinary, but was covered with a layer of soft, handmade carpet.
Ever since he was young, Fangzheng had never lived in a room like this.
He immediately nodded, "This is very good, really not bad.
Thank you, Shen Mama. "
This Shen Mama was his aunt's most valued servant. She managed all the servants in the house, and was truly a housekeeper.
Fang Yuan's personal maidservant, Shen Cui, was her daughter.
Shen Mama chuckled, "How could this servant dare to accept Young Master's thanks? It's what I should do, what I should do!
Young Master, you can eat and sleep well. If you want anything, just ring the bell by the bedside, and a servant will immediately come up to listen to your orders.
Master has instructed that for these days, Young Master, you should wholeheartedly focus on your cultivation. The other trivial matters will be handled by us servants. "
Fangzheng's heart once again surged with gratitude. He didn't say anything else, but silently made up his mind: This time, he must win first place, and not let Uncle and Aunt down!
…
The dark clouds in the sky were getting thicker, and the night was getting darker.
The stars in the night sky were almost completely covered by the clouds, leaving only a few flickering weakly in the sky.
"Uncle and Aunt must be planning on how to kick me out of the house.
In my previous life, they secretly instigated servants to provoke me, then framed me, and finally kicked me out of the house. I don't know how things will change in this life. "Fang Yuan walked on the street, laughing coldly in his heart.
He had long seen through his uncle and aunt's true colors.
But it was understandable.
People die for wealth, and birds die for food. Whether it was on Earth or in this world, there were always so many people who trampled on family, friendship, and love for the sake of profit.
In reality, there was no such thing as family.
When his uncle and aunt adopted Fang Yuan and Fangzheng, their main purpose was to covet the inheritance.
It was just that the two brothers, Fang Yuan and Fangzheng, had repeatedly surprised them.
"Everything is difficult at the beginning, especially for me.
First, I don't have an outstanding aptitude, and second, I don't have a teacher to take care of me. It's the same as starting from scratch.
My parents' inheritance can be said to be a huge springboard for me.
In my previous life, my inheritance was taken away by my uncle and aunt, causing me to spend a whole two years before I could cultivate to the peak of the first rank.
In this life, I can't make this mistake again. "
Fang Yuan walked like this, thinking at the same time.
He did not stay in his residence, but carried two jars of wine, heading out of the village.
The night sky was getting darker and darker, and the dark clouds covered the stars. The mountain wind was blowing, and it seemed to be getting stronger.
A storm was brewing.
But he still had to explore.
If he wanted to get back his parents' inheritance, he would have to wait until he turned sixteen next year.
And the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance was something that could be obtained in the near future.
There were very few people on the streets.
Dim light shone from the houses by the side of the road. Some trivial household rubbish, leaves, and dust were blown by the wind, drifting about randomly.
Fang Yuan's thin clothes could not withstand the mountain wind, and he could not help but feel a chill.
He simply opened the jar of wine he was carrying, and took a small sip.
Although it was turbid, but after swallowing it, there was a warm feeling rising in his body.
This was the first time he really drank wine in these few days.
The further he went out of the village, the fewer the houses by the side of the road, and the dimmer the lights.
In front of him, it was even darker.
The wind blew against the forest, and the branches swayed in the night, making whooshing sounds, like the roar of a group of beasts.
Fang Yuan's steps did not have the slightest bit of hesitation, and he went out of the village's main gate, walking further and further away in the dark road.
And behind him, was the bright and glorious lights of tens of thousands of houses.
In the midst of these lights, there was a warm corner.
His younger brother, Guyue Fangzheng, sat in front of his desk, revising the notes he had taken in class.
The house was brightly lit, and the solid walls blocked the cold wind. Beside his hand was a cup of warm ginseng tea, steam rising in spirals.
"Young Master Fangzheng, the hot water for bathing has been prepared for you." Outside the door, Shen Cui's voice came softly.
Fangzheng's heart moved, "Then bring it in."
With a charming face, Shen Cui swayed her waist as she walked into the room.
"This servant greets Young Master Fangzheng." She looked at Fangzheng with flirtatious eyes.
Fang Yuan was only a C grade talent, while Fangzheng was an A grade talent.
To be able to climb up to him, that was the true wealth!
S-REV-012.summary: As the red sun sets, Fang Yuan's uncle and aunt attempt to formalize the adoption of both Fang Yuan and Fangzheng, offering Yuan stones to both. Fangzheng eagerly accepts, grateful for their perceived kindness, while Fang Yuan, sensing their ulterior motives regarding his inheritance, silently rejects the offer. After Fang Yuan leaves, the uncle and aunt reveal their plan to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him to secure his parents' wealth, demonstrating their malicious intent.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan understands the process and challenges of Gu refinement, including the will of the Gu and the need for primeval essence replenishment.
Fang Yuan recognizes the severe limitations of his C-grade aptitude in Gu refinement and primeval essence recovery compared to higher aptitudes.
Fang Yuan understands that a lack of Yuan stones severely hinders his ability to refine Gu efficiently.

S-REV-013.prose:
Chapter 10
Pa pa pa …
Bean-sized raindrops fell densely.
The raindrops hit the verdant bamboo building, making a crisp sound.
In the pond in front of the building, the surface of the water was beaten by the raindrops. Fish swam happily in the water, and water plants swayed at the bottom of the pond.
Dark clouds covered the sky, and the entire field of vision was blocked by a thick curtain of rain.
In a dark room, the window was open. Fang Yuan quietly watched the heavy rain, sighing in his heart, "It's already been three days and three nights."
Three days ago, he carried two jars of wine and left the village, continuing to explore the surroundings.
But late at night, it rained cats and dogs.
Let's not talk about him being drenched like a drowned rat. The key was that in such a situation, he could no longer explore around.
The rain could quickly wash away the smell of alcohol, and at the same time, if he forcefully explored in the rain, he might arouse suspicion.
Previously, he pretended to be frustrated and drunk to hide his true motives.
But never underestimate the intelligence of others. Often, only fools would think that others were stupid.
So in desperation, Fang Yuan could only stop his exploration.
And this rain continued. During this period, it was heavy or light, sparse or dense, but it never stopped.
"This way, I won't be able to find the Liquor worm in a short period of time.
To be safe, I can only start refining the Moonlight Gu.
In this refining process, it would be best if I can find the Liquor worm, but if I can't, then I can only make do with it.
But this is very normal. There are unpredictable winds and clouds in the sky.
Is there anyone in this world who can always do things smoothly and perfectly? "
Fang Yuan's state of mind was very calm. Five hundred years of experience had long washed away the impulsiveness in his temperament.
He closed the window and door, sitting cross-legged on the bed.
He slowly closed his eyes, and after regulating his breathing, he sank his mind.
The next moment, the scene of his own aperture appeared in his mind.
Although the aperture was in the body, it was very mysterious, infinitely big and infinitely small.
On the outside of the aperture, there was a layer of light.
The white light gave a very thin feeling, but it firmly supported the aperture.
In the aperture, there was a primeval sea.
The sea was bronze in color, and the surface was as calm as a mirror. The water level was about half of the aperture's height.
The entire volume of the primeval sea occupied 44% of the aperture.
This was a Rank one Gu Master's bronze primeval sea. Every drop of seawater was primeval essence.
It was Fang Yuan's life force, the condensation of Fang Yuan's essence.
Every drop of primeval essence was precious, because it was a Gu Master's foundation, the source of power.
Using primeval essence, Gu Masters could refine and activate Gu worms..
As his mind left the primeval sea, Fang Yuan opened his eyes and took out the Moonlight Gu.
The Moonlight Gu quietly lay in Fang Yuan 'heart., "Gu" Moonlight Gu. Gu "Fang" Fang "Gu" Fang ".
"Bang." Bai light. of Gu Moonlight. it "Gu" in., "Gu". Gu, Fang Yuan, and, suddenly. the sea. "Moonlight.
. the sea. sea. "Gu"., and, and blue light. in. in. in. "Moonlight" in. "." Gu "Gu." Gu "in.". "
"Gu" Gu ". is." Gu was. were. is..
. It was a living creature. It was born free. It had its own will.
Fang Yuan wanted to refine it. He wanted to erase its will. Sensing the danger, the Moonlight Gu resisted.
The process of refining was very difficult.
The Moonlight Gu was like a crescent moon. When the bronze primeval essence was injected into the crescent moon, the two tips of the crescent moon were dyed green.
Then, the bronze green color started to spread to the middle of the crescent moon.
In less than three minutes, Fang Yuan's face was pale.
Large amounts of primeval essence kept pouring into the Moonlight Gu. It caused a feeling of weakness, like the meridians were being pulled out.
One percent, two percent, three percent … eight percent, nine percent, ten percent.
Ten minutes later, Fang Yuan's primeval sea had used up ten percent of his primeval essence.
But on the surface of the blue crystal-like Moonlight Gu, the two bronze green dots at the crescent moon only expanded a little.
The Moonlight Gu's resistance was very tenacious.
Fortunately, Fang Yuan had expected this. He was not surprised. He continued to inject primeval essence into the Moonlight Gu.
Ten percent, twenty percent, thirty percent.
After another twenty minutes, Fang Yuan's primeval sea was only left with fourteen percent. The bronze green dots on the Moonlight Gu expanded a little. If the two green dots were superimposed, it would be about one-twelfth of the surface of the Moonlight Gu.
As for the rest, it was still the light blue color of the Moonlight Gu.
"Refining a Gu is difficult." Seeing this, Fang Yuan sighed. He stopped supplying primeval essence and stopped refining the Moonlight Gu.
At this moment, refining the Gu for half an hour had consumed more than half of the primeval sea. There was only fourteen percent of primeval essence left.
And the Moonlight Gu had only been refined by one-twelfth.
Moreover, what was even more difficult to accept was that the Moonlight Gu was still emitting a faint blue halo.
Although Fang Yuan stopped refining, it did not stop resisting. It was still expelling Fang Yuan's bronze primeval essence.
Fang Yuan could clearly feel that the primeval essence that had been injected into the Moonlight Gu was being expelled out of the body bit by bit.
On the surface of the Moonlight Gu, the bronze green dots on the two crescent tips were also slowly shrinking.
Based on this rate of reduction, after about six hours, the Moonlight Gu would be able to expel all of Fang Yuan's primeval essence.
At that time, refining this Gu would be no different from sacrificing it all over again.
"Every time I refine a Gu, it is like two armies fighting a battle of position, a war of attrition.
I have only refined one-twelfth of the Gu, but my primeval essence has been consumed by a full thirty percent.
When refining a Gu, a Gu Master must replenish the primeval essence in the primeval sea while continuously sacrificing to consolidate the results of victory.
Refining a Gu is not only a test of the technique of mobilizing primeval essence, but also the patience of a prolonged battle. "
Fang Yuan thought as he took out a Yuan stone from his money bag.
There were usually two ways for a Gu Master to replenish primeval essence.
The first was natural recovery.
Every once in a while, the primeval sea would automatically replenish primeval essence.
For a C grade talent like Fang Yuan, it would take about an hour to replenish forty percent of primeval essence.
In six hours, he could recover twenty-four percent of his total primeval essence.
The second method was to absorb the natural primeval energy in the Yuan stone.
The Yuan stone was a treasure of nature, condensing natural primeval essence and could be absorbed by a Gu Master.
Fang Yuan held the Yuan stone in his hand, continuously absorbing the natural primeval essence from within, into the primeval sea in his aperture.
The fine luster on the surface of the Yuan stone slowly dimmed, while the water level in Fang Yuan's primeval sea rose at a speed visible to the naked eye.
About half an hour later, the primeval sea had recovered to forty-four percent of its original size.
At this point, the rising trend of the sea level came to an abrupt halt.
Although there was still space in the aperture, Fang Yuan could no longer store any more primeval essence.
This was the limitation of his C grade talent.
From this, one could see the importance of cultivation talent.
The higher the talent, the more primeval essence could be stored in the aperture, and the faster the natural recovery of primeval essence.
For Fang Yuan, to refine the Gu and consolidate the results, he had to absorb the Yuan stone. This was because the natural recovery speed of his primeval essence could not be compared to the speed at which the Moonlight Gu expelled primeval essence.
But for an A grade talent like Fangzheng, he could replenish eighty percent of primeval essence every hour.
In six hours, he could recover forty-eight percent of his total primeval essence, while the Moonlight Gu could only expel thirty percent of primeval essence in the same six hours.
He did not even need external help like the Yuan stone. He could continue refining like this, rest a few times in between, and after a few days, he would be able to successfully refine the Moonlight Gu.
Therefore, from the beginning, Fang Yuan knew that in this assessment of refining the Moonlight Gu, he had no chance of getting first place.
This had nothing to do with strength, but talent was the first factor.
The second factor was the Yuan stone.
If there was an abundance of Yuan stone, without hesitation, a B grade talent could also surpass an A grade talent and get first place.
"I only have six pieces of Yuan stone in my hands. I can't compare to people like Guyue Mobei and Guyue Cheng, who have the support of elders behind them.
My talent is only C grade, and I can't compare to an A grade talent like Guyue Fangzheng.
There was no chance of winning this assessment, so I might as well divert my energy to look for the Liquor worm.
If I could refine the Liquor worm into my vital Gu, it would be much better than the Moonlight Gu.
Hmm?
The sound of the rain outside the window had lessened, and there seemed to be signs of it stopping.
This rain has been going on for three days and three nights, it's time to stop. "
Fang Yuan put away the Moonlight Gu, got off the bed, and was about to open the window when there was a knock on the door.
The voice of his personal maid, Shen Cui, came from outside the door. "Young master Fang Yuan, it's me.
It's been raining for three days, so I brought you some food and wine. Young master, eat and drink a little, and it will relieve some of your boredom. "
S-REV-013.summary: Fang Yuan reflects on three days of continuous rain, which has stalled his exploration for the Liquor worm. He decides to focus on refining the Moonlight Gu, acknowledging the difficulty due to his C-grade aptitude and limited Yuan stones. The refinement process proves arduous, consuming much of his primeval essence, and highlighting the severe disadvantage of his talent compared to A-grade Gu Masters like Fangzheng.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan learns that Shen Cui is involved in his uncle and aunt's plot to frame him.
Shen Cui realizes Fang Yuan is aware of the framing plot and is a dangerous, ruthless individual.
Shen Cui is used by Uncle and Aunt Guyue to seduce and frame Fang Yuan.
Shen Cui is the daughter of Shen Mama and serves as Fang Yuan's personal maid.

C-REV-01.transition: left his residence and went to the inn

S-REV-014.prose:
Chapter 11
Fang Yuan's brows slightly wrinkled. Based on his intuition and 500 years of life experience, he smelled a conspiracy.
A cold light flashed in his eyes and his brows relaxed: "I'm a little hungry. You came at the right time, bring it in for me."
Outside the door, Shen Cui carried the food box. When she heard this, her mouth revealed a trace of disdain.
But when she pushed open the door, there was only a gentle look left on her face.
"Young Master Fang Yuan, this food and wine is very fragrant. This servant can smell it through the food box." Her voice was sweet and greasy, revealing an amorous charm.
Putting the food box on the small table, Shen Cui took out the plates one by one and arranged them.
The plates were indeed full of delicious food.
She then took out two wine glasses and poured wine.
"Come, Young Master, sit down.
This servant boldly wants to drink with Young Master today. "
She smiled like a flower and walked to Fang Yuan's side. She boldly pulled Fang Yuan's hand and dragged him to the stool next to the table.
Then she directly sat on Fang Yuan's thighs. Her delicate body leaned on Fang Yuan's chest and she said in Fang Yuan's ear: "Young Master Fang Yuan, this servant has always liked you.
No matter what aptitude, this servant wants to accompany you, rely on you, and comfort you.
Tonight, this servant wants to give my body to you. "
Today, she was dressed up.
She put on rouge and her lips were like cherry pink. Because she was speaking close to his ear, a delicate and youthful breath teased Fang Yuan's earlobe.
Because she was sitting in his arms, Fang Yuan could clearly feel Shen Cui's plump body.
Her elastic thighs, her soft waist, and the softness of her chest.
"Young Master, let this servant feed you wine." Shen Cui picked up the wine glass, raised her head, and sipped the wine.
Then, her eyes seemed to be filled with water as she stared at Fang Yuan. Her cherry mouth was slightly open as she slowly leaned towards Fang Yuan's lips.
Fang Yuan's expression was cold, as if the person in his arms was not a girl, but a statue.
Looking at Fang Yuan's expression, Shen Cui's heart was a little uneasy at first. But when her lips were only a finger's distance away from touching Fang Yuan's lips, she was sure and smiled disdainfully in her heart: "Still pretending."
Right at this moment, Fang Yuan sneered and said in a disdainful tone, "So it's just seduction."
Shen Cui's expression stiffened. She swallowed the wine in her mouth and pretended to be angry: "Young Master Fang Yuan, what are you saying?"
Fang Yuan's pair of eyes faintly emitted a cold light, staring at Shen Cui's eyes. At the same time, his right hand was placed on her snow-white neck, slowly exerting force.
Shen Cui's pupils shrank and her voice was filled with panic: "Young Master, you are hurting me."
Fang Yuan did not reply, but the strength in his hands increased.
"Young Master Fang Yuan, this servant is a little scared!"
Shen Cui was already somewhat out of breath. She looked flustered and a pair of delicate hands subconsciously placed on Fang Yuan's hands, wanting to pry his hands away.
But Fang Yuan's hands were like iron pincers, how could she pry them away?
"It seems that Uncle and Aunt let you seduce and frame me?
In that case, there should be people arranged downstairs. "Fang Yuan sneered contemptuously," But who do you think you are to seduce me?
Just based on the two piles of rotten flesh on your chest. "
Saying that, his left hand climbed onto Shen Cui's chest and fiercely pinched the softness on her chest. All of a sudden, it violently deformed.
A strong pain came from her chest. Shen Cui's eyes widened and tears filled her eyes. She wanted to cry out, but her throat was choked by Fang Yuan. In the end, she could only whimper a few times. She began to strongly resist. If she didn't resist, she would really suffocate!
But at this time, Fang Yuan slowly loosened his grip.
Shen Cui immediately opened her mouth and greedily breathed air. Her breathing was too urgent, so much so that it caused a violent dry cough.
Fang Yuan chuckled softly. He stretched out his palm and gently stroked Shen Cui's cheek. He leisurely said, "Shen Cui, do you think I can kill you?"
If Fang Yuan had roared and roared, Shen Cui might have fiercely resisted.
But when Fang Yuan smiled and spoke softly, asking her if he could kill her in such a soft voice, Shen Cui felt a heartfelt fear.
She was afraid!
She looked at Fang Yuan in horror. She looked at this young man who was smiling at her.
At this moment, Shen Cui swore that she would never forget Fang Yuan's eyes.
This pair of eyes was not mixed with the slightest emotion. They were dark and deep, like an ancient pond that hid a terrifying giant beast.
Under the gaze of this pair of eyes, Shen Cui felt as if she was naked and placed in a world of ice and snow!
The person in front of her absolutely dared to kill her. He could kill her …
Oh my god!
Why did I come to provoke such a devil?!
Shen Cui's heart was filled with regret. At this moment, she wanted to turn around and escape.
But at this moment, she threw herself into Fang Yuan's arms. She did not dare to escape. She did not even dare to make a single movement.
All the muscles in her body were tense. Her delicate body trembled. Her face was as pale as paper. She could not say a word.
"Considering that you have served me for so many years as my personal maid, I will not kill you this time.
Don't you want to get rid of your slave status? Go find my younger brother. He is stupid and naive. "Fang Yuan put away his smile and patted Shen Cui's cheek. His tone was as calm as water.
With a sigh, he finally said —
"You can go."
Shen Cui was dumbstruck and obediently walked out.
She was dazed. She did not know how she escaped from the side of Fang Yuan, this devil.
The people hidden in the dark, seeing Shen Cui come out like this, looked at each other in confusion.
"You actually arranged a beauty trap. This is a new idea compared to the previous life.
Hehe, Uncle and Aunt, I will deeply remember your kindness! "
Not long after Shen Cui left, Fang Yuan stood up and went out the door.
No matter what, he could not stay in this residence.
A gentleman does not stand under a collapsing wall. Not to mention a devil.
Without enough strength, only a fool would put himself in danger.
"Shopkeeper, do you have a room?" Coming to the only inn in the village, Fang Yuan asked for the price.
"Yes, yes. There are rooms on the second and third floor. Not only are they cheap, they are also very clean.
The first floor is the dining hall. You can eat here, or you can ask the waiter to send food to your room. "The shopkeeper attentively served Fang Yuan.
This inn was the only one in the village. Business was not good. It seemed a bit deserted.
Only when the caravan came to Qing Mao Mountain to trade every year would the inn be full of people.
Fang Yuan was really hungry, so he tossed two whole primeval stones to the shopkeeper: "Give me a room, I'll stay in it first. Also, prepare two jars of wine and three or four dishes, I'll refund any excess and make up for any deficiency."
"Okay."
The shopkeeper took the two pieces of Yuan stone and asked, "Would you like to eat in your room or in the dining hall?"
Fang Yuan looked at the sky. The rain had already stopped and it was almost evening.
He might as well finish eating in the dining hall and then leave the village to explore the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance.
He said to the shopkeeper: "Let's eat in the dining hall."
The inn had a dining hall on the first floor. There were more than ten square tables and four long benches around the tables.
Between the tables, there were thick pillars supporting the inn.
The floor was covered with pieces of marble, but it was wet and could not hide the humidity in the mountains.
There were three tables in the dining hall.
At the table by the window, there was only an old man drinking wine. He looked at the sunset outside the window and slowly drank alone.
At the table in the middle, there were five or six hunters sitting in a circle. They were loudly talking about their hunting experiences. There was a pile of pheasants and hares on the ground beside their feet.
At the table in the corner, there were two young people who seemed to be secretly discussing something.
Their figures were hidden in the shadows and could not be seen clearly. It was impossible to tell if they were men or women.
Fang Yuan chose a seat near the door and sat down. Not long after, food and wine were served on the table.
"With my C grade talent, I need the help of Yuan stone to refine the Moonlight Gu.
If my luck is good and the Moonlight Gu's will is not strong, I only need five pieces.
If it is stubborn, then it will be troublesome. I need at least eight pieces. "
Gu worms were also living beings and naturally had the will to live.
Some had strong willpower and would keep resisting the Gu Master's refinement.
Some had weak willpower and would surrender at the end of the refinement. Once it lost the will to resist, then the refinement would be extremely easy.
"I only have six pieces of Yuan stone on me now. I've already given two to the shopkeeper, so I only have four left.
It's not enough. "
In this world, Yuan stone was a hard currency and had a strong purchasing power.
For a mortal family of three, their monthly living expenses would at most be one primeval stone.
But to a Gu Master, the consumption of Yuan stone was huge.
Just like Fang Yuan. Just refining the Gu required an average of seven pieces of Yuan stone.
And this was just the Moonlight Gu. If he really found the Liquor worm and wanted to refine it, with Fang Yuan's talent, he would need to spend at least ten more pieces of Yuan stone!
"That is to say, the current situation is — even if I find the Liquor worm, I may not have Yuan stone to help me refine it.
But I still need to continue exploring because the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance is very likely to have large amounts of Yuan stone. "
This was not hard to deduce.
The Flower Wine Monk was a Rank five Gu Master, a famous demonic expert. How could he not have Yuan stone, a Gu Master's essential cultivation item?
S-REV-014.summary: Shen Cui, Fang Yuan's maid, enters his room with food and wine, attempting to seduce him as part of Uncle and Aunt Guyue's scheme to frame him. Fang Yuan, having anticipated this, coldly thwarts her advances, intimidating her with physical force and revealing his awareness of the plot. He then dismisses her, advising her to seek Fangzheng instead, and decides he can no longer safely stay in the residence.

continuityMutations.content: Fang Yuan realizes that his current Yuan stone supply is insufficient for refining the Moonlight Gu, let alone the Liquor worm if he finds it.
Fang Yuan solidifies his belief that the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance is crucial for acquiring the necessary Yuan stones.

S-REV-015.prose:
Chapter 11
Fang Yuan's brows slightly wrinkled. Based on his intuition and 500 years of life experience, he smelled a conspiracy.
A cold light flashed in his eyes and his brows relaxed: "I'm a little hungry. You came at the right time, bring it in for me."
Outside the door, Shen Cui carried the food box. When she heard this, her mouth revealed a trace of disdain.
But when she pushed open the door, there was only a gentle look left on her face.
"Young Master Fang Yuan, this food and wine is very fragrant. This servant can smell it through the food box." Her voice was sweet and greasy, revealing an amorous charm.
Putting the food box on the small table, Shen Cui took out the plates one by one and arranged them.
The plates were indeed full of delicious food.
She then took out two wine glasses and poured wine.
"Come, Young Master, sit down.
This servant boldly wants to drink with Young Master today. "
She smiled like a flower and walked to Fang Yuan's side. She boldly pulled Fang Yuan's hand and dragged him to the stool next to the table.
Then she directly sat on Fang Yuan's thighs. Her delicate body leaned on Fang Yuan's chest and she said in Fang Yuan's ear: "Young Master Fang Yuan, this servant has always liked you.
No matter what aptitude, this servant wants to accompany you, rely on you, and comfort you.
Tonight, this servant wants to give my body to you. "
Today, she was dressed up.
She put on rouge and her lips were like cherry pink. Because she was speaking close to his ear, a delicate and youthful breath teased Fang Yuan's earlobe.
Because she was sitting in his arms, Fang Yuan could clearly feel Shen Cui's plump body.
Her elastic thighs, her soft waist, and the softness of her chest.
"Young Master, let this servant feed you wine." Shen Cui picked up the wine glass, raised her head, and sipped the wine.
Then, her eyes seemed to be filled with water as she stared at Fang Yuan. Her cherry mouth was slightly open as she slowly leaned towards Fang Yuan's lips.
Fang Yuan's expression was cold, as if the person in his arms was not a girl, but a statue.
Looking at Fang Yuan's expression, Shen Cui's heart was a little uneasy at first. But when her lips were only a finger's distance away from touching Fang Yuan's lips, she was sure and smiled disdainfully in her heart: "Still pretending."
Right at this moment, Fang Yuan sneered and said in a disdainful tone, "So it's just seduction."
Shen Cui's expression stiffened. She swallowed the wine in her mouth and pretended to be angry: "Young Master Fang Yuan, what are you saying?"
Fang Yuan's pair of eyes faintly emitted a cold light, staring at Shen Cui's eyes. At the same time, his right hand was placed on her snow-white neck, slowly exerting force.
Shen Cui's pupils shrank and her voice was filled with panic: "Young Master, you are hurting me."
Fang Yuan did not reply, but the strength in his hands increased.
"Young Master Fang Yuan, this servant is a little scared!"
Shen Cui was already somewhat out of breath. She looked flustered and a pair of delicate hands subconsciously placed on Fang Yuan's hands, wanting to pry his hands away.
But Fang Yuan's hands were like iron pincers, how could she pry them away?
"It seems that Uncle and Aunt let you seduce and frame me?
In that case, there should be people arranged downstairs. "Fang Yuan sneered contemptuously," But who do you think you are to seduce me?
Just based on the two piles of rotten flesh on your chest. "
Saying that, his left hand climbed onto Shen Cui's chest and fiercely pinched the softness on her chest. All of a sudden, it violently deformed.
A strong pain came from her chest. Shen Cui's eyes widened and tears filled her eyes. She wanted to cry out, but her throat was choked by Fang Yuan. In the end, she could only whimper a few times. She began to strongly resist. If she didn't resist, she would really suffocate!
But at this time, Fang Yuan slowly loosened his grip.
Shen Cui immediately opened her mouth and greedily breathed air. Her breathing was too urgent, so much so that it caused a violent dry cough.
Fang Yuan chuckled softly. He stretched out his palm and gently stroked Shen Cui's cheek. He leisurely said, "Shen Cui, do you think I can kill you?"
If Fang Yuan had roared and roared, Shen Cui might have fiercely resisted.
But when Fang Yuan smiled and spoke softly, asking her if he could kill her in such a soft voice, Shen Cui felt a heartfelt fear.
She was afraid!
She looked at Fang Yuan in horror. She looked at this young man who was smiling at her.
At this moment, Shen Cui swore that she would never forget Fang Yuan's eyes.
This pair of eyes was not mixed with the slightest emotion. They were dark and deep, like an ancient pond that hid a terrifying giant beast.
Under the gaze of this pair of eyes, Shen Cui felt as if she was naked and placed in a world of ice and snow!
The person in front of her absolutely dared to kill her. He could kill her …
Oh my god!
Why did I come to provoke such a devil?!
Shen Cui's heart was filled with regret. At this moment, she wanted to turn around and escape.
But at this moment, she threw herself into Fang Yuan's arms. She did not dare to escape. She did not even dare to make a single movement.
All the muscles in her body were tense. Her delicate body trembled. Her face was as pale as paper. She could not say a word.
"Considering that you have served me for so many years as my personal maid, I will not kill you this time.
Don't you want to get rid of your slave status? Go find my younger brother. He is stupid and naive. "Fang Yuan put away his smile and patted Shen Cui's cheek. His tone was as calm as water.
With a sigh, he finally said —
"You can go."
Shen Cui was dumbstruck and obediently walked out.
She was dazed. She did not know how she escaped from the side of Fang Yuan, this devil.
The people hidden in the dark, seeing Shen Cui come out like this, looked at each other in confusion.
"You actually arranged a beauty trap. This is a new idea compared to the previous life.
Hehe, Uncle and Aunt, I will deeply remember your kindness! "
Not long after Shen Cui left, Fang Yuan stood up and went out the door.
No matter what, he could not stay in this residence.
A gentleman does not stand under a collapsing wall. Not to mention a devil.
Without enough strength, only a fool would put himself in danger.
"Shopkeeper, do you have a room?" Coming to the only inn in the village, Fang Yuan asked for the price.
"Yes, yes. There are rooms on the second and third floor. Not only are they cheap, they are also very clean.
The first floor is the dining hall. You can eat here, or you can ask the waiter to send food to your room. "The shopkeeper attentively served Fang Yuan.
This inn was the only one in the village. Business was not good. It seemed a bit deserted.
Only when the caravan came to Qing Mao Mountain to trade every year would the inn be full of people.
Fang Yuan was really hungry, so he tossed two whole primeval stones to the shopkeeper: "Give me a room, I'll stay in it first. Also, prepare two jars of wine and three or four dishes, I'll refund any excess and make up for any deficiency."
"Okay."
The shopkeeper took the two pieces of Yuan stone and asked, "Would you like to eat in your room or in the dining hall?"
Fang Yuan looked at the sky. The rain had already stopped and it was almost evening.
He might as well finish eating in the dining hall and then leave the village to explore the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance.
He said to the shopkeeper: "Let's eat in the dining hall."
The inn had a dining hall on the first floor. There were more than ten square tables and four long benches around the tables.
Between the tables, there were thick pillars supporting the inn.
The floor was covered with pieces of marble, but it was wet and could not hide the humidity in the mountains.
There were three tables in the dining hall.
At the table by the window, there was only an old man drinking wine. He looked at the sunset outside the window and slowly drank alone.
At the table in the middle, there were five or six hunters sitting in a circle. They were loudly talking about their hunting experiences. There was a pile of pheasants and hares on the ground beside their feet.
At the table in the corner, there were two young people who seemed to be secretly discussing something.
Their figures were hidden in the shadows and could not be seen clearly. It was impossible to tell if they were men or women.
Fang Yuan chose a seat near the door and sat down. Not long after, food and wine were served on the table.
"With my C grade talent, I need the help of Yuan stone to refine the Moonlight Gu.
If my luck is good and the Moonlight Gu's will is not strong, I only need five pieces.
If it is stubborn, then it will be troublesome. I need at least eight pieces. "
Gu worms were also living beings and naturally had the will to live.
Some had strong willpower and would keep resisting the Gu Master's refinement.
Some had weak willpower and would surrender at the end of the refinement. Once it lost the will to resist, then the refinement would be extremely easy.
"I only have six pieces of Yuan stone on me now. I've already given two to the shopkeeper, so I only have four left.
It's not enough. "
In this world, Yuan stone was a hard currency and had a strong purchasing power.
For a mortal family of three, their monthly living expenses would at most be one primeval stone.
But to a Gu Master, the consumption of Yuan stone was huge.
Just like Fang Yuan. Just refining the Gu required an average of seven pieces of Yuan stone.
And this was just the Moonlight Gu. If he really found the Liquor worm and wanted to refine it, with Fang Yuan's talent, he would need to spend at least ten more pieces of Yuan stone!
"That is to say, the current situation is — even if I find the Liquor worm, I may not have Yuan stone to help me refine it.
But I still need to continue exploring because the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance is very likely to have large amounts of Yuan stone. "
This was not hard to deduce.
The Flower Wine Monk was a Rank five Gu Master, a famous demonic expert. How could he not have Yuan stone, a Gu Master's essential cultivation item?
S-REV-015.summary: Fang Yuan secures a room at the village inn after thwarting Shen Cui's attempt to frame him. While eating in the dining hall, he reflects on his limited Yuan stones and the high cost of Gu refinement, recognizing that even if he finds the Liquor worm, he might not have the resources to refine it. This reinforces his determination to pursue the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance, which he believes contains the necessary wealth.

continuityMutations.content: The Gu Master Jiang Ya's rank (Rank One) and D-grade aptitude based on his attire and primeval essence.
Fang Yuan is also a Gu Master, specifically a junior in the academy, due to his primeval essence aura.
Jiang Ya is a Gu Master, instilling fear.
Fang Yuan is also a Gu Master.
Believes the Flower Wine Monk's treasure will solve his problems and plans to use Green Bamboo Wine to attract a Liquor worm.
Prefers high-risk, high-reward strategies, especially when low on resources, but knows when to cut losses. Views the world as 'big fish eats small fish'.
Possesses knowledge of a future event where a Gu Master found the Flower Wine Monk's remains after getting drunk and attracting a Liquor worm, and uses this knowledge to guide his current actions.
Suspects the official clan story of the Flower Wine Monk's death and treasure discovery is incomplete or misleading due to a logical loophole.
Has spent most of his Yuan stones on wine and acknowledges the risk and potential impact on his cultivation if he fails.
Tries to smooth over a tense situation with the waiter and later with the young Gu Master.
Skilled at handling rowdy customers and recognizing when a sale won't happen, later fawns over Gu Masters.
Boasts about the rarity and quality of Green Bamboo Wine.
Uses his Gu Master status to intimidate and assert dominance over mortals.
Is concerned about lacking 'source stones' (Yuan stones for Gu Masters), indicating personal financial strain despite his status.
Believes mortals are inferior and unworthy of flaunting wealth in front of Gu Masters.
Advises Jiang Ya to show restraint and not make things difficult for mortals.
His death and the location of his treasure are central to Fang Yuan's current quest, with conflicting historical accounts.
Fought and killed the Flower Wine Monk, but died shortly after from injuries; his story is a central part of Guyue clan lore.

C-REV-01.transition: Walked out of the inn with the jar of wine

S-REV-016.prose:
Chapter 12 "Right now, all the problems are related to the Flower Wine Monk's treasure. If I can find it, all the problems will be solved. If I can't find it, these problems will greatly slow down my training. It will cause me to be left far behind by my peers at the beginning of my training. I don't understand, I've used more than a week to attract the Liquor worm, why is there no result? " Fang Yuan furrowed his brows, thinking hard. He did not know what the food in his mouth tasted like. Just at this moment, a wave of noise came over and interrupted his thoughts. Fang Yuan looked towards the direction of the noise, and found that it came from the table in the middle. Six hunters were seated around the table, already drunk. The atmosphere was heated, and all of them were red in the face. "Brother Zhang, come, let's drink another cup!" "Brother Feng, we admire your ability. You single-handedly took care of a black-skinned wild boar, what a hero! You must drink this cup of wine. If you don't, you won't be giving us face. " "Thank you for your appreciation, but I really can't drink anymore." "Brother Feng can't drink anymore, is it because the wine is not good enough? Waiter, come over here, give us some good wine! " The noise became louder and louder, and it was obvious that the hunters had drunk too much. The waiter quickly walked over, "Brothers, we do have good wine, but it's a little expensive." "What, are you afraid that we won't pay?!" The hunters heard this, and a few stood up, glaring at the waiter. They were either tall and burly, or they were dark, skinny, and muscular. They were capable and forceful, and they had the unique valiance of the mountain people. The waiter quickly greeted them, and complained, "How would I dare to look down on you heroes?

It's just that this wine is really expensive, one jar costs two Yuan stone!" The hunters were stunned. Two Yuan stone, that was not cheap, it was the living expenses of a normal family for two months. Hunters could earn more from hunting than mortals. Sometimes, a black-skinned wild boar would be worth half a yuan stone. However, hunting was also risky. Sometimes, one might become the prey if one was not careful. To a hunter, spending two spirit stones for a jug of wine was really not worth it. "Is there really such an expensive wine?" "Kid, you're not lying to us, are you?" The hunters were shouting and yelling, but their voices were a little weak and awkward. The child didn't even dare to cry out. The hunter called Brother Feng saw that the situation wasn't right and quickly tried to smooth things over, "Brothers, let's not waste any more money. I can't drink anymore today. Let's drink another day. " "What are you talking about, Big Brother?" "That won't do …" The rest of the hunters continued to shout, but their voices had already weakened. One by one, they returned to their seats. The waiter was also an astute person. Looking at the situation, he knew that the deal couldn't be done. However, he was already used to this kind of situation. Just as he was about to retreat, the voice of a young man suddenly came from a table in the corner. "Hehe, how funny. What are you all making such a fuss about? If you can't afford to buy wine, then shut up and shrink to the side!" When the hunters heard this, one of them was immediately provoked and yelled, "Who said we can't afford it?

Child, bring that jar of wine. I'll give you Yuan stone. It's only two!" "Ah, please wait a moment. It'll be served soon!" The waiter didn't expect this turn of events and immediately responded. He turned around and brought up a jar of wine. This jar of wine was only half the size of an ordinary jar of wine. However, when the seal was removed, a mellow fragrance of wine wafted out and filled the entire dining hall. The old man who was sitting by the window and drinking alone couldn't help but turn his head because of the fragrance of the wine and cast his gaze on the jar of wine. It was indeed a good wine. "Dear customers, I'm not bragging. This is the best Green Bamboo Wine. In the entire village, our inn is the only one that has it. Smell this wine! "As the waiter spoke, he took a deep breath. His face was full of enjoyment and satisfaction. Fang Yuan's heart moved. This waiter wasn't bragging after all. There were three taverns in the village of Guyue. They all sold ordinary rice wine and turbid wine. They were all similar. In order to attract the Liquor worm, Fang Yuan had continuously bought wine for seven days. Naturally, he was aware of the market. Several hunters looked at the jar of wine in front of them. They were all addicted to wine.

One by one, their noses twitched and their Adam's apple moved. As for the hunter who blurted out and bought the wine, his expression became even more interesting. There was a hint of annoyance on his face. This jar of wine was worth two Yuan stone! "I bought this wine on impulse. This waiter is too unprofessional. He immediately served the wine. Now that the seal has been removed, I can't return it even if I want to. " The more the hunter thought about it, the more his heart ached. He wanted to return it, but he couldn't bring himself to do so. Finally, he slammed the table and forced a smile, "Damn, this wine is good! Brothers, drink to your heart's content. Today's wine is on me! " At this moment, the young man at the table in the corner sneered, "How can this small jar of wine be enough for six people? If you have the guts, buy a few more jars. " The hunter's veins bulged at these words. He stood up abruptly and flew into a rage. He stared at the young man who spoke, "Little bastard, you talk a lot. Come, stand up and spar with me! " "Oh? Then I'll stand up. "When the young man heard this, he really stood up and walked out from the shadows of the corner with a sinister smile. He was tall and thin, his face pale. He wore a dark blue martial attire, looking clean and neat. He wore a sapphire blue headband on his head and a short shirt, revealing his thin shoulders. He wore long pants and bamboo shoes.

There were also leggings around his calves. Most importantly, he wore a green belt around his waist. In the middle of the belt was a shiny copper plate. On the copper plate, there was a black "One" carved on it. "A Rank one Gu Master?!" The hunter who shouted obviously understood the meaning of this attire. He sucked in a breath of cold air. The anger on his face faded and turned into fear. He did not think that he would provoke a Gu Master! "Didn't you want to spar with me? Come, make your move. "The young Gu Master walked over with a mocking smile on his face. But the hunter who provoked him just now was like a statue, standing there motionless. "Or all of you can come at me together." The young Gu Master slowly walked to the hunter's table and said casually. The hunters' faces changed. Some whose faces were red from drinking turned pale in an instant. Cold sweat dripped from their foreheads. They were restless and did not dare to breathe loudly. The young Gu Master stretched out a hand and lifted the jar of green bamboo wine. He put it under his nose and smelled it, laughing, "It's really fragrant …" "If Lord Gu Master likes it, you can take it and drink it. Just treat it as my apology for offending you. "The hunter who provoked and shouted hurriedly cupped his hands and bowed, his face full of smiles. Unexpectedly, the young Gu Master's expression suddenly changed. With a bang, he threw the wine jar to the ground. The Gu Master's face was like ice, his gaze like a sword.

He growled in a low voice, "You think you are qualified to apologize to me? You hunters are really rich, richer than me. You actually spent two pieces of Yuan stone to buy wine to drink?! Don't you know that I've been worrying about source stones! How dare you flaunt your wealth in front of me at this time! You mortals are not worthy?! " "We dare not, we dare not!" "We offended Lord, we deserve to die!" "We did not mean to offend you. This is the Yuan stone on us, please accept it, Lord Gu Master." The hunters all stood up as if they were electrocuted, taking out Yuan stone from their bosoms. But these mortals did not have much money. They only took out bits and pieces of Yuan stone, the biggest one was not more than a quarter. The young Gu Master did not take the Yuan stone. He just kept laughing coldly, using his hawk-like gaze to scan the whole dining hall. The hunters who were scanned by him all lowered their heads. The old men who were watching by the window also quickly turned their heads, avoiding the Gu Master's gaze. Only Fang Yuan watched quietly, without any scruples. This young Gu Master's attire could only be worn by an official Gu Master. Even Fang Yuan did not have the qualifications. Only after Fang Yuan graduated from the academy could he receive it from the clan. The young Gu Master's belt had the word "1" on it, indicating his status as a Rank one Gu Master. But he already looked like he was in his twenties, and the primeval essence aura he emitted should be Rank one upper stage. He started cultivating at the age of fifteen, and was still Rank one upper stage when he was in his twenties. This meant that the young man's aptitude was only D grade, even lower than Fang Yuan. It was very possible that he was just a logistics Gu Master, not even a battle Gu Master.

But even so, it was more than enough to deal with the six strong hunters. This was the difference in strength between a Gu Master and a mortal. "With power, you can be high and mighty. This is the nature of this world. No, any world is like this, the big fish eats the small fish, the small fish eats the shrimps. It's just that this world is more naked. "Fang Yuan sighed in his heart. "Alright Jiang Ya, just teach them a lesson, don't make things difficult for these mortals. Even if you don't find it shameful, I would." Another young man sitting in the corner said. When everyone heard her speak, they realized that this young man was a woman. The young Gu Master named Jiang Ya was displeased by his female companion's words, and stopped sneering. He did not even look at the scattered Yuan stone that the hunters offered. These Yuan stone added up was not even enough for two pieces, so of course he had no interest. He flicked his sleeves and walked back to his original seat. As he walked, he said some harsh words, "If you have the guts, continue drinking. Drink the green bamboo wine. I want to see who still dares to drink this wine. " The hunters lowered their heads, reprimanded like six obedient grandsons. The rich aroma of the wine permeated the entire dining hall. The hunter who bought the wine smelled the aroma, his face twitching in pain. He had spent two pieces of Yuan stone on this wine, but he did not even get to drink a sip! Fang Yuan put down his chopsticks, he was already full. Smelling the aroma of the wine, his gaze flashed a few times, and he suddenly took out two pieces of Yuan stone and placed them on the table, saying indifferently, "Waiter, give me a jar of green bamboo wine." Everyone was stunned. The young Gu Master Jiang Ya stopped in his tracks, his mouth twitching as he let out a breath.

He had just said some harsh words, and Fang Yuan asked for this jar of wine. Wasn't this purposely tearing down his stage and slapping his face? He turned around and squinted his eyes, shooting a cold glare at Fang Yuan. Fang Yuan stared back at him calmly, his face indifferent and without fear. Jiang Ya's gaze flashed, and the cold air gradually faded. He could feel the primeval essence aura on Fang Yuan's body. He knew Fang Yuan's identity, and immediately smiled warmly like a spring breeze, "So it's a junior." The others came to a realization, and the way they looked at Fang Yuan had already changed. No wonder this young man was not afraid of Gu Masters, he was also a Gu Master. Although he was still in school, his essence was already different. "Lord Gu Master, here's your wine!" The waiter ran over with a fawning smile. Fang Yuan nodded to the young Gu Master Jiang Ya, carrying the jar of wine and walking out of the inn. ================================================== Chapter 13 About three hundred years ago, a genius appeared in the Guyue clan. He was brimming with talent. He was still a young man, but he had already cultivated to the level of a Rank five Gu Master, with the possibility of advancing further. His name spread throughout Qing Mao Mountain, and he had boundless glory. In the history of the Gu Yue clan, he was the fourth generation clan leader who was most famous. Unfortunately, in order to protect his clansmen, the fourth generation clan leader fought against the Rank five Gu Master demon, the Flower Wine Monk. After an intense battle, he defeated the Flower Wine Monk and made the demon kneel and beg for mercy. But in the end, the fourth generation clan leader was careless, and was sneakily attacked by the Flower Wine Monk. The fourth generation clan leader angrily killed the Flower Wine Monk, but he himself was heavily injured and died young. This tragic story had been passed down to this day, and was widely spread by the Guyue clan. But Fang Yuan understood that this story could not be completely believed. Because there was a huge loophole in it. In his previous life, a month later, a Gu Master who got drunk due to a breakup laid dead outside the village.
S-REV-016.summary: Fang Yuan is deep in thought about his quest for the Flower Wine Monk's treasure when a group of drunken hunters cause a disturbance, refusing to pay for expensive wine. A young Gu Master named Jiang Ya intervenes, humiliating the hunters and asserting his dominance. Fang Yuan, unfazed, then orders the same expensive wine, subtly challenging Jiang Ya and revealing himself to be a Gu Master.

continuityMutations.content: The Liquor worm's presence confirms his strategy for finding the Flower Wine Monk's treasure is viable.

S-REV-017.prose:
In the end, the smell of wine attracted a Liquor worm. The Gu Master chased the Liquor worm, and found the remains of the Flower Wine Monk in a secret underground cave, as well as his personal belongings. The Gu Master hurried back to the clan and reported the matter, causing a huge commotion. After the commotion died down, he also benefited from it. He obtained the Liquor worm, his cultivation increased, and the girlfriend who abandoned him chased him instead. This made him a famous person in the village. Stories were passed down from generation to generation, so it was normal for it to change. But in Fang Yuan's memories, although the story of the Gu Master finding the treasure was extremely believable, he suspected that there might be other truths hidden in it. "I didn't notice it at first, but these days I've been exploring and analyzing, and found something fishy about it."
