Suddenly, the Liquor worm undergoes a violent backlash, curling into a ball and emitting a dazzling light, sending a powerful will into Fang Yuan's primeval sea. As Fang Yuan prepares to resist, the legendary Spring Autumn Cicada mysteriously appears in his aperture, emanating an overwhelming aura, leaving him completely shocked.

continuityMutations.content: The Spring Autumn Cicada is his true lifebound Gu, capable of suppressing other Gu worms even in its weakened state.
He can use the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura to easily refine lower-rank Gu worms, circumventing his C-grade talent limitations.
It is a Rank 6 Gu, Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, severely weakened from reversing time but still highly potent.
Its presence as Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, despite his low rank, is an unprecedented anomaly.
He possesses the Spring Autumn Cicada (Rank 6), his lifebound Gu, which is severely weakened but still potent enough to suppress other Gu worms.
He realizes the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura can significantly ease the refinement of lower-rank Gu worms, enabling him to overcome his C-grade talent limitations.
He defies the academy's expectations and common belief by getting first place in Gu refinement despite his C-grade talent.
It is revealed to be the mechanism of Fang Yuan's rebirth, having reversed 500 years of time at great cost to its vitality.
It is a Rank 6 Gu, Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, currently in a severely weakened and dormant state but capable of emitting an intimidating aura.
Its presence as Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, despite his Rank 1 status and the Cicada's Rank 6, is an unprecedented anomaly.

S-REV-026.prose:
Chapter 19
During the refining process, he suffered a backlash from the Gu worm!
The Liquor worm inherited the Flower Wine Monk's strong will, and now invaded the aperture, brazenly attacking Fang Yuan.
This strong will power, from top to bottom, fiercely attacked the Green Bronze Essence Sea at the bottom of the aperture.
And the Green Bronze Essence Sea was also tumbling, setting off bursts of waves.
Under Fang Yuan's will, a large amount of primeval essence rose up against the heavens, converging together, forming a wild wave that soared to the sky, brazenly facing the Liquor worm's will.
The two sides were about to fiercely collide in the center of the aperture.
But at this moment, in the center of the two forces, in the empty space of the aperture, a faint shadow of a Gu worm appeared.
It was a cicada.
This cicada was not big. If the Moonlight Gu was a blue crystal curved like the moon, then this cicada was like a master craftsman made of palm wood and leaves.
Its head and abdomen were brownish yellow, and the surface had tree-ring like veins, as if witnessing the passage of time.
The wings on its back were very wide, translucent, like two overlapping leaves.
Both wings had veins, and they were very similar.
These veins were like the typical net-like veins of a leaf. In the center was a thick stem, and from the stem radiated a network of leaf veins on both sides.
The Spring Autumn Cicada!
It was startled.
It was like a giant beast, usually hiding in a cave and sleeping.
But it was suddenly awakened, and found that its territory had been invaded.
Who dares to come to my territory and behave atrociously!
As if its dignity had been violated, the Spring Autumn Cicada was angry, and it emitted an aura.
This aura was weak, but it was so strong.
It was like a torrential milky way, rolling forward, the waves vast and mighty, sweeping thousands of miles of green mountains, drowning the vast desert!
Compared to this aura, the Liquor worm's will was like an ant seeing an elephant!
The aura swept and expanded in all directions, like a formless and peerless tsunami.
The Liquor worm's invading will, upon meeting this tsunami, did not even have the ability to resist, and was directly swallowed up by this aura.
Fang Yuan's chest tightened.
He tried his best to stir up the green bronze primeval essence, and upon meeting this aura, it was like a wave crashing into a mountain.
In an instant, the condensed primeval essence disintegrated, collapsing into rain, falling back into the primeval sea.
Whoosh.
Waves rose and fell on the surface of the True Essence Sea, as if a storm had fallen, adding to the turbulence.
But after a few breaths, the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura spread out and pressed down on the Primal Sea.
Boom!
Fang Yuan seemed to hear a buzzing sound. In an instant, the billowing Primordial Sea came to a standstill.
The Spring Autumn Cicada's aura firmly suppressed the entire Primordial Sea, like an invisible mountain pressing down. The surface of the sea did not have the slightest ripple, and was as calm as a mirror.
It was like a piece of wrinkled paper had been covered and flattened by the giant hand formed by the aura.
This was an unparalleled power!
Fang Yuan felt as if there was an invisible mountain pressing down on his heart. It was as if Sun Wukong was being pressed under the Five Elements Mountain. He couldn't mobilize even a trace of True Essence.
However, even though he was shocked, he wasn't afraid. Instead, a strand of intense joy surged into his heart.
"I didn't expect the Spring and Autumn Cicada to be reborn with me!
So it is not a one-time consumable Gu, but can be used repeatedly. "
The Spring and Autumn Cicada was a Rank six Gu. It was Fang Yuan's first and last Rank six Gu in his previous life.
In order to refine it, Fang Yuan had used all his means and resources. He had expended tremendous effort and thirty years of preparation before he was lucky enough to succeed.
But not long after he succeeded, before the Spring and Autumn Cicada had even warmed up, the heroes of the righteous path felt the threat of Fang Yuan. They attacked together and killed Fang Yuan.
After rebirth, Fang Yuan didn't find the Spring and Autumn Cicada and thought it had died.
But in fact, it was residing in Fang Yuan's body and had fallen into a deep slumber.
It had suddenly reversed five hundred years of time, and its vitality was greatly damaged. It was too weak, so weak that even its owner, Fang Yuan, could not sense it.
Even though the Spring and Autumn Cicada had appeared, the situation was still not good.
Since rebirth, it had been resting in a deep slumber.
It appeared now only because it sensed the danger in the aperture and was awakened by the Liquor worm's will.
It was very weak, very weak, extremely weak.
In Fang Yuan's memory, the original Spring and Autumn Cicada was full of vitality. Its body was like expensive floorboards, emitting a warm and glossy sheen.
Its wings were light green, like two fresh leaves that had just sprouted.
But at this moment, a strong aura of death emanated from the cicada's body.
Its body did not have any luster, and looked rough and dim, like dead wood.
Its wings were also not light green leaves, but were full of a withered yellow colour, like withered leaves that were about to wither in autumn.
And the tips of its wings were slightly curled up and incomplete, like the edges of fallen leaves.
Regarding this, Fang Yuan felt both heartache and thankful.
His heartache was that the Spring and Autumn Cicada had suffered such a heavy injury and was only a step away from death, like one foot on the edge of a cliff.
Thankfully, the Spring and Autumn Cicada was so weak, or else he would be in big trouble!
After all, Gu Masters and Gu worms had to complement each other, and it was best if they were of the same realm.
A Rank one Gu Master would use a Rank one Gu worm, this was the most suitable.
If the Gu worm's rank was lower than the Gu Master's, the Gu Master would be like a strong man carrying a small wooden stick, very weak.
If the Gu worm's rank was higher than the Gu Master's, the Gu Master would be like a child carrying a heavy axe, willing but unable to do so.
The Spring and Autumn Cicada was a Rank six Gu, and Fang Yuan was only a Rank one initial stage Gu Master.
To make an analogy, the Spring and Autumn Cicada was a mountain, and Fang Yuan was a squirrel.
If the squirrel were to carry the mountain to smash the enemy, the squirrel would be squashed by the mountain immediately.
If the Spring and Autumn Cicada was at its peak, Fang Yuan's small Rank one Gu Master's aperture would not be able to contain it, and would directly burst to death from its majestic aura.
Fortunately, it was extremely weak, so Fang Yuan's aperture could contain it.
"I gave up on the Moonlight Gu, and tried all ways and means to find the Liquor worm, just to refine the lifebound Gu.
But actually, I already have the lifebound Gu, the Spring and Autumn Cicada is my lifebound Gu! "Feeling the intimate connection between himself and the Spring and Autumn Cicada, Fang Yuan's heart was filled with emotions.
The lifebound Gu was the first Gu that a Gu Master refined. It was very important, and to a large extent, it affected the Gu Master's future development.
The better the lifebound Gu was, the smoother the Gu Master's development would be.
A poor quality lifebound Gu would drag down the Gu Master's cultivation, and they would be overtaken by their peers.
Most importantly, it would affect the life and death of a battle.
Fang Yuan was well aware of this, so even though he chose the Guyue Village's Moonlight Gu, he was still not satisfied.
He tried all ways and means to find the Liquor worm.
In his memory, to a new Rank one Gu Master, the Liquor worm was already a superior choice.
The Moonlight Gu could only be considered above average.
But life was fascinating because people never knew what was waiting for them in the next moment.
In his previous life, Fang Yuan refined the Spring and Autumn Cicada.
After his rebirth, the Spring and Autumn Cicada fell into a deep slumber, but this connection with the refinement still existed.
And it seemed that after going through the River of Time, Fang Yuan discovered that the connection between him and the Spring and Autumn Cicada was even more mysterious and closer than in his previous life.
It was only because the Spring and Autumn Cicada was too weak that Fang Yuan did not notice it.
So, in a real sense, the Spring and Autumn Cicada was the first Gu he refined.
But the Spring and Autumn Cicada was not refined by Fang Yuan in this life, but the result of his five hundred years of hard work in his previous life.
The Spring and Autumn Cicada was Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu.
A Rank one Gu Master with a Rank six lifebound Gu!
If this was said out, probably no one would believe it!
This had already broken the limits of common knowledge!
But this matter, had actually happened.
The truth was unquestionable.
"The Liquor worm is the best choice for a lifebound Gu, but compared to the Spring and Autumn Cicada, it is like mud on the ground!
My lifebound Gu in this life is actually the Spring and Autumn Cicada, hahaha … "

==================================================
S-REV-026.summary: While attempting to refine the Liquor worm, Fang Yuan experiences a backlash from its will. Unexpectedly, the dormant Spring Autumn Cicada awakens, its powerful aura effortlessly suppressing both the Liquor worm's will and Fang Yuan's primeval essence. Fang Yuan realizes the Spring Autumn Cicada, his true lifebound Gu, is severely weakened but still a formidable asset. He then uses its aura to easily refine both the Liquor worm and the Moonlight Gu, securing his first-place position in the academy and re-evaluating his cultivation strategy.

continuityMutations.content: He learns that Fang Yuan, a C-grade talent, has achieved first place in Moonlight Gu refinement, defying all his expectations.
He is now publicly recognized as the first-place refiner, despite his C-grade talent.
He initially assumes Fangzheng, an A-grade talent, is the first to refine a Moonlight Gu, reflecting a strong bias towards talent over effort.
He is utterly astonished and perplexed by Fang Yuan's C-grade talent achieving first place.

C-REV-01.transition: Walked to the academy

S-REV-027.prose:
Chapter 20
The great joy did not go to Fang Yuan's head. He quickly calmed down and began to think about the Spring Autumn Cicada's influence on him:
"The Spring Autumn Cicada's ability is rebirth.
But it is extremely weak now. Once it is used, it will die.
But it is a Rank six Gu after all. I can use its aura.
There is no harm done to it. "
"Hahaha." Thinking of this, Fang Yuan withdrew his mind and opened his eyes.
In front of him, the Liquor worm was floating in front of him, wrapped in a smoky green bronze primordial essence, shivering.
Just now in order to survive, it staked everything on one throw. The result was that its will was easily defeated by the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura.
It was also seriously injured, and its will was less than one percent of what it was before.
"Spring Autumn Cicada." Fang Yuan's mind moved, releasing a trace of the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura.
The aura oppressed the Liquor worm, and it immediately became still, motionless, as if it was dead.
Its remaining will, feeling the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura, was like a mouse meeting a cat. It was completely intimidated, shrinking into a ball and not daring to move.
Fang Yuan laughed, taking the opportunity to stimulate his primordial essence.
When the green bronze primordial essence was first refined, because of the Liquor worm's stubborn resistance, it could only expand bit by bit.
But at this moment, Fang Yuan's green bronze primordial essence rushed straight in, flowing a thousand miles.
It did not encounter any hindrance at all.
The surface of the Liquor worm's bronze color rapidly expanded. In a few blinks of an eye, the white pearl-like Liquor worm was dyed green.
The momentum was lost. The Liquor worm's remnant will was finally gently rushed by Fang Yuan's will, and immediately disintegrated.
Just like this, the Liquor worm was refined!
Compared to the previous arduous process of traversing mountains and ridges, this refinement process was as simple as drinking water.
A familiar and mysterious feeling connected the Liquor worm and Fang Yuan together.
The refined Liquor worm was like a part of Fang Yuan's body.
When Fang Yuan told it to curl up, it would curl up. When he told it to curl up, it would curl up into a ball.
It felt like he was moving his fingers.
Fang Yuan withdrew his primordial essence, and the Liquor worm returned to its white and chubby appearance.
Then, it leapt into the air and entered Fang Yuan's aperture.
Inside the aperture, it avoided the Spring Autumn Cicada in the air and entered the Green Bronze Primordial Sea.
On the surface of the sea, it wantonly stretched its body, twisting its chubby waist from time to time. It was so comfortable as if it was soaking in a hot bath.
"With the Spring Autumn Cicada, I should change my plans." Fang Yuan withdrew his consciousness from his aperture and took out the Moonlight Gu.
He did the same thing and released a trace of the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura, suppressing the Moonlight Gu.
Feeling the Spring Autumn Cicada's aura, the Moonlight Gu's will immediately laid down its arms and surrendered. It was so afraid that it could only hide in the corner of its body.
Fang Yuan's true essence rolled down, and in the blink of an eye, the Moonlight Gu was dyed into an emerald jade.
Finally, with just a slight thought, the Moonlight Gu's will was easily destroyed.
After that, he withdrew his primeval essence, and the Moonlight Gu returned to its previous transparent blue crystal appearance.
He kept the Moonlight Gu.
This Moonlight Gu did not enter the aperture, but fell directly in the middle of Fang Yuan's forehead, forming a light blue crescent moon imprint.
From the beginning to the end of refining the Moonlight Gu, the whole process took less than five minutes.
This was a stark contrast to the difficulty of the refinement process and the speed of the refinement.
Not only was the speed fast, but the consumption of primeval essence was also extremely low.
These days, Fang Yuan had used up six Yuan stones to refine the Liquor worm.
But this night, he did not use any Yuan stones at all. It was only the Green Bronze Primordial Sea in his aperture that was almost empty.
"Haha, with the Spring Autumn Cicada in hand, it's like the help of God!
From now on, as long as I use its aura to suppress any Rank one Gu, it will be easy to capture any Rank one Gu.
Even if I only have C grade talent, I don't need the help of the Yuan stone.
The difference before and after is like heaven and earth. "
Fang Yuan was in a good mood. At this moment, it was as if the clouds had parted and he could see the blue sky.
Even though the Spring Autumn Cicada was extremely weak, it was still a dignified Rank six Gu.
Just by relying on its aura, it would be a huge driving force for Fang Yuan's future cultivation.
At this moment.
Outside the window, the moon was bright and the stars were sparse. The moonlight passed through the window and shone on Fang Yuan's face.
"I thought I wouldn't get first place, but who would have thought there would be such a turn of events.
Time waits for no man. I'll go to the academy now and receive the first prize! "Fang Yuan's eyes flashed.
With a thought, the Spring Autumn Cicada in his aperture concealed its figure and disappeared again, entering deep sleep.
Then he summoned the Liquor worm and hid it in the corner of the bed.
This was to prevent the academy from conducting unnecessary inspections.
Fifteen minutes later, the family academy.
The academy elder had already fallen asleep, but in his dreams, he vaguely heard someone knocking on the door.
He was awakened by the knocking. Opening his eyes, he was somewhat displeased, "It's the middle of the night, who is outside?"
A respectful reply came from outside the door, "Reporting to Sir Elder, it's a student from this year's batch. He has already successfully refined the Moonlight Gu.
You instructed your subordinates to report to you immediately as soon as the first place appears, no matter what time it is. "
"Hmm … there is such a thing."
The academy elder frowned and got off the bed. As he put on his clothes, he asked, "Which student is this year's first place?
Is it Guyue Fangzheng? "
The subordinate outside the door answered, "It seems so.
I heard the news and immediately came to report to you, sir. It seems to be the Fang family branch. "
"Heh, looking at the time, it should be him." The academy elder laughed softly and muttered to himself, "Other than this A grade genius, who else could it be?
Those B grade students, even if they used the Yuan stone, are still lacking by a level.
Otherwise, why is the aptitude for cultivation so important? "
Saying so, he pushed the door open and walked out.
Outside the door, the subordinate respectfully took two steps back and bowed, echoing, "Sir is right."
In the hall, more than ten candles burned together, brightly illuminating the hall.
The person who received Fang Yuan had already asked about the situation.
Under the bright light, his face was filled with shock, "Wait, what did you just say? Your name is Guyue Fang Yuan, not Guyue Fangzheng?"
Fang Yuan nodded. At this moment, the academy elder walked in from outside the door.
Fang Yuan and the person who received him turned and bowed together.
The academy elder saw Fang Yuan, and his face was full of smiles.
He walked in front of Fang Yuan and patted Fang Yuan's shoulder in a friendly manner, "Well done, Guyue Fangzheng, you didn't let me down.
You really are a genius with A grade talent!
Those B grade and C grade peers, no matter how hard they work, can't compare to you.
Hahaha. "
Fang Yuan and Fangzheng were twins, and their appearances were so similar that the academy elder mistook them for each other.
Fang Yuan was neither servile nor overbearing, and took a step back, freeing his shoulder from the academy elder's palm.
He looked at the academy elder, his hands behind his back, and smiled lightly, "Sir elder, you've mistaken me for someone else.
I am Guyue Fang Yuan, and Guyue Fangzheng is my younger brother. "
"Hmm?" The academy elder opened his mouth slightly, and a look of astonishment appeared on his face.
He looked at Fang Yuan, puzzled, and his brows furrowed.
After a few breaths, he opened his mouth, "You are Guyue Fang Yuan?"
"That's me," Fang Yuan answered.
"You've refined the Moonlight Gu?"
The academy elder was astonished. His eyes stared closely at the crescent moon mark on Fang Yuan's forehead, his eyes shining, and he already knew the answer.
"That's what happened," Fang Yuan answered again.
"So, you're this year's first?" The academy elder's question seemed a little silly, but he could not be blamed.
This was because the situation was too unexpected.
He had been in charge of the academy for decades, and was extremely experienced.
He had seen C grade talents take first place, but it was definitely not this early.
Moreover, in this year, there were A grade and B grade peers.
"If there's no one earlier than me …" Fang Yuan pretended to think, and then rubbed his nose before continuing, "Seems like it."
Academy elder, "…"

==================================================
S-REV-027.summary: An academy subordinate wakes the Academy Elder late at night to report that a student has successfully refined the Moonlight Gu and claimed first place. The Elder assumes it's Fangzheng (A-grade talent), revealing his ingrained bias. However, upon meeting the student, he is shocked to discover it is Fang Yuan (C-grade talent), who calmly confirms his achievement, leaving the Elder astonished and questioning the unprecedented turn of events.

continuityMutations.content: He learns that Fang Yuan, his C-grade brother, has surprisingly achieved first place in Moonlight Gu refinement.
He feels a deep sense of injustice and resentment that Fang Yuan, whom he views as lazy and inferior, has surpassed him.
He is convinced he will be the first to refine the Moonlight Gu due to his A-grade talent and hard work.
He is utterly shocked and disbelieving when he learns Fang Yuan, his C-grade brother, achieved first place.
He harbors deep resentment and incomprehension towards Fang Yuan's unexpected success, questioning the fairness of the world.
He recalls past negative interactions and feelings of being suppressed by Fang Yuan.
They mistake Fangzheng for Fang Yuan due to their similar appearance.

C-REV-09.transition: Walked quickly through the morning streets

S-REV-028.prose:
Chapter 21
The sky had yet to brighten, and the sun had yet to rise. The eastern sky was just beginning to turn white.
The darkness in the sky gradually faded, and the air was still filled with the fragrance of the night.
The sound of light footsteps could be heard on the almost empty street.
The morning in the mountains was always cold and wet, but Guyue Fangzheng did not feel the slightest bit cold. His heart was filled with surging excitement.
His face was flushed, and he was walking quickly towards the school.
"These days, I've been training hard, and I've spent two Yuan stone. I didn't sleep at all last night, and I've finally succeeded in refining the Moonlight Gu.
I have A grade talent, and I'm so hardworking.
No one can be faster than me, no one!
Father, mother, I said I won't let you down. "
Thinking of his uncle and aunt's happy and gratified expressions when he told them the good news, Fangzheng felt a burst of heartfelt happiness and pride.
"Just you wait, those clansmen who looked down on me, and my brother.
From today onwards, I'll make you look at me, Guyue Fangzheng, in a new light! "
The more Fangzheng thought about it, the more excited he became. He could not help but clench his fists, and his footsteps became lighter.
He came to the school entrance.
The two guards guarding the school saw him, but they were puzzled, and asked him, "Hm, Guyue Fang Yuan, why are you back?"
"Why, did brother just come here?" Fangzheng heard this, and his face flashed a trace of surprise and doubt.
"Let's not talk about this for now!"
He did not expect Fang Yuan to win first place. He shook his head, and with a hint of pride, he said, "Brothers, I'm not Guyue Fang Yuan, I'm Guyue Fangzheng.
I've already successfully refined the Natal Gu, and this time I'm here to receive the first place reward. "
"You're Guyue Fangzheng?
You two brothers look so alike, no wonder the elder mistook you. "The guard on the left widened his eyes and shouted.
The guard on the right shook his head and said, "You're a step too late. Last night, your brother Guyue Fang Yuan had already met with the Clan Elder and received the first prize."
"My brother!" Fangzheng's eyes widened and he cried out involuntarily, "Wait, you said he won first place?"
How was this possible?
Isn't Big Brother only a C grade talent?
First place? Are you kidding me?!
"It's true.
Why would we joke about such a thing? "Seeing that Fangzheng did not believe him, the guard was a little displeased.
"This matter has already been confirmed by the academy elder.
The ranking list would be released soon.
Why, didn't your brother tell you about this? "Another guard immediately added.
Fangzheng was dumbfounded. He stood at the door in a daze.
The truth was so different from what he had expected that he couldn't even figure out what had happened.
In Fangzheng's mind, there were also a few imaginary formidable opponents.
Among them, the two most threatening ones were Guyue Mobei and Guyue Cheng.
Both of them had B grade aptitude and were backed by the two largest branch families in the clan. Their grandfathers were elders in power and had sufficient financial resources.
If these two people won first place before him, Fangzheng would have been mentally prepared. Although he would have been disappointed, he could still accept it.
But now, the one who won first place was not Guyue Mobei, nor was it Guyue Cheng. It wasn't even any of the imaginary enemies in his mind.
It was Guyue Fang Yuan, his brother!
That C grade talent!
That person who had fallen into depravity after the Awakening Ceremony!
That person who slept soundly in class all day long!
That person who drank too much and didn't return home at night!
That person who bullied Shen Cui, slapped him twice, and took all the Yuan stone he had!
That person who had been suppressing him in the past, like a shadow entrenched in his heart!
"How could this be?
This can't be! "
For a moment, Fangzheng roared in his heart, "I worked so hard, but he drank too much all day long. In the end, he still won first place. Is there any justice left?
Why?
Why?! "
The sun rose from the east. Birds chirped and chirped. Spring swept across Qing Mao Mountain.
Guyue Fangzheng bathed in the warm sunlight. He slowly lowered his head, gritted his teeth, and looked at his lonely shadow on the ground.
The surging emotions in his heart were like a deflated balloon. There wasn't much left.
What replaced them were doubts, resentment, incomprehension, unwillingness, fear, and other complex emotions.
…
As time passed, the sun climbed higher and higher.
A new list was posted on the school's bulletin board. There were only two names on the list. They were Fang Yuan and Fangzheng.
Following the appearance of this list, the news gradually spread.
Those youths who had wholeheartedly stayed at home to refine Gu after receiving their Gu worms were in an uproar after hearing the news.
"How could this be!"
"If it was Fangzheng who got first, I would still believe it. But Fang Yuan, isn't he C grade talent?"
"Are you kidding me? The A grade talent Fangzheng actually lost to the C grade talent Fang Yuan. Is this a fantasy?"
The Mo family.
The small courtyard was full of greenery, and the fragrance of tea wafted in the air.
One of the elders in charge of the Guyue family, Guyue Mochen, sat in front of his desk. He looked at the spring scenery outside the window and drank his tea leisurely. "Hasn't Mobei continued refining the Gu?"
The housekeeper standing at the side quickly replied, "Ever since he heard the news about Fang Yuan in the morning, Young Master Mobei seemed to have suffered a huge blow. He no longer has the mood to continue refining the Moonlight Gu.
Speaking of which, it's a pity that Young Master Mobei was so close to success.
Actually, if Fangzheng got first place, it would be fine. But it just had to be that C grade talent Fang Yuan.
So it's no wonder that Young Master Mobei lost interest. "
"Hmph!
Don't try to excuse him. "
Guyue Mochen snorted coldly. His face was solemn and his tone was steely. "A Gu Master's cultivation is full of hardships. What is this little setback?
That Fang Yuan is only a C grade talent. The reason he got first place was probably due to luck.
The Moonlight Gu he chose had weak willpower, allowing him to beat him to it.
If Mobei can't see through this and can't even handle this setback, then how will he be able to control our Mo family in the future? How will he be able to compete with the Chi family?
No one is allowed to persuade him. Let him think about it carefully! "
"Yes, master." The housekeeper did not dare to refute.
Almost at the same time, in the Chi family.
"Sigh, Guyue Fang Yuan …" The elder Guyue Lian sighed deeply. He frowned and thought for a while. He waved his hand and said, "Someone, call Young Master Chicheng in."
Not long after, Guyue Cheng walked into the room with a face full of disappointment. He knelt down and said, "Grandchild greets Grandpa."
"Looks like you already know about this news."
Guyue Lian stared at his only grandson. His tone was soft and said slowly, "I called you here to prevent you from being affected by this matter.
Refining the natal Gu depends on one's aptitude and the Gu worm. "
"Fang Yuan's aptitude is only C grade, but he was able to get first place. This means that the Gu worm he chose, compared to the Moonlight Gu in your hands, has a much weaker willpower.
This is all luck.
So grandson, don't be discouraged. This is nothing.
He is only C grade. Although he is the same as you, he doesn't have as many resources as you do. His road to promotion will be harder than yours. Believe me, you will soon surpass him. "
"So, let go of this insignificant matter.
Fang Yuan will not be your rival, nor is he worthy of being called your rival.
Your real rival is the A grade Fangzheng, the Mobei of the Mo family.
Do you understand? "
"Yes, thank you grandpa for enlightening me. I understand.
I will go down now and continue refining the Gu! "The disappointment on Guyue Cheng's face disappeared. What replaced it was a spirited fighting spirit.
"Okay."
The elder Guyue Lian nodded his head in satisfaction. He showed a kind and kind smile, "Good grandson, although your true aptitude is only C grade, don't worry, I will support you with all my strength.
Later, I will use the aura of a Rank 3 Gu to suppress the Moonlight Gu's willpower and help you refine this Gu! "
S-REV-028.summary: Fangzheng, filled with pride and excitement after successfully refining his Moonlight Gu, arrives at the academy entrance, expecting to claim first place. The guards mistake him for Fang Yuan and reveal that his brother has already won. This news shatters Fangzheng's confidence, leaving him dumbfounded and consumed by disbelief, resentment, and a profound sense of injustice that his C-grade brother surpassed him.

continuityMutations.content: He views Fang Yuan's first-place achievement as a fluke and a test of character for Mobei, rather than a genuine challenge to the traditional aptitude system.
He has lost motivation to refine his Gu after hearing of Fang Yuan's success.
He is a demanding and strict mentor to Guyue Mobei, seeing Fang Yuan's success as a mere stroke of luck and a test of character for Mobei.
He holds a traditional view on Gu Master aptitude and cultivation progression, dismissing Fang Yuan's achievement as an anomaly rather than a challenge to his beliefs.
He loses the will to continue refining his Gu after hearing of Fang Yuan's first-place achievement.

S-REV-029.summary: News of Fang Yuan's unexpected first-place victory spreads, causing an uproar among the other students. In the Mo family courtyard, Elder Guyue Mochen learns that his grandson, Mobei, is disheartened and has stopped refining his Gu. Mochen coldly dismisses Fang Yuan's success as mere luck and a test for Mobei's character, asserting that true talent and resilience are paramount for future leadership, effectively reinforcing traditional views on aptitude.

continuityMutations.content: He plans to use a Rank 3 Gu's aura to help Guyue Cheng refine his Moonlight Gu, demonstrating his willingness to use his power to aid his favored grandson.
He is re-motivated by his grandfather's words and assistance, regaining his fighting spirit.
He is a supportive grandfather, offering guidance and practical help to Guyue Cheng.
He reassures Guyue Cheng, reframing Fang Yuan's success as an insignificant fluke and emphasizing the importance of focusing on true rivals.
He offers to use a Rank 3 Gu's aura to help Guyue Cheng refine his Moonlight Gu, demonstrating a willingness to leverage power for his grandson's benefit.
He is initially disappointed by the news of Fang Yuan's success.
He regains his fighting spirit after his grandfather's encouragement and strategic advice.

C-REV-17.transition: Walked into the room upon summons

S-REV-030.prose:
Chapter 21
The sky had yet to brighten, and the sun had yet to rise. The eastern sky was just beginning to turn white.
The darkness in the sky gradually faded, and the air was still filled with the fragrance of the night.
The sound of light footsteps could be heard on the almost empty street.
The morning in the mountains was always cold and wet, but Guyue Fangzheng did not feel the slightest bit cold. His heart was filled with surging excitement.
His face was flushed, and he was walking quickly towards the school.
"These days, I've been training hard, and I've spent two Yuan stone. I didn't sleep at all last night, and I've finally succeeded in refining the Moonlight Gu.
I have A grade talent, and I'm so hardworking.
No one can be faster than me, no one!
Father, mother, I said I won't let you down. "
Thinking of his uncle and aunt's happy and gratified expressions when he told them the good news, Fangzheng felt a burst of heartfelt happiness and pride.
"Just you wait, those clansmen who looked down on me, and my brother.
From today onwards, I'll make you look at me, Guyue Fangzheng, in a new light! "
The more Fangzheng thought about it, the more excited he became. He could not help but clench his fists, and his footsteps became lighter.
He came to the school entrance.
The two guards guarding the school saw him, but they were puzzled, and asked him, "Hm, Guyue Fang Yuan, why are you back?"
"Why, did brother just come here?" Fangzheng heard this, and his face flashed a trace of surprise and doubt.
"Let's not talk about this for now!"
He did not expect Fang Yuan to win first place. He shook his head, and with a hint of pride, he said, "Brothers, I'm not Guyue Fang Yuan, I'm Guyue Fangzheng.
I've already successfully refined the Natal Gu, and this time I'm here to receive the first place reward. "
"You're Guyue Fangzheng?
You two brothers look so alike, no wonder the elder mistook you. "The guard on the left widened his eyes and shouted.
The guard on the right shook his head and said, "You're a step too late. Last night, your brother Guyue Fang Yuan had already met with the Clan Elder and received the first prize."
"My brother!" Fangzheng's eyes widened and he cried out involuntarily, "Wait, you said he won first place?"
How was this possible?
Isn't Big Brother only a C grade talent?
First place? Are you kidding me?!
"It's true.
Why would we joke about such a thing? "Seeing that Fangzheng did not believe him, the guard was a little displeased.
"This matter has already been confirmed by the academy elder.
The ranking list would be released soon.
Why, didn't your brother tell you about this? "Another guard immediately added.
Fangzheng was dumbfounded. He stood at the door in a daze.
The truth was so different from what he had expected that he couldn't even figure out what had happened.
In Fangzheng's mind, there were also a few imaginary formidable opponents.
Among them, the two most threatening ones were Guyue Mobei and Guyue Cheng.
Both of them had B grade aptitude and were backed by the two largest branch families in the clan. Their grandfathers were elders in power and had sufficient financial resources.
If these two people won first place before him, Fangzheng would have been mentally prepared. Although he would have been disappointed, he could still accept it.
But now, the one who won first place was not Guyue Mobei, nor was it Guyue Cheng. It wasn't even any of the imaginary enemies in his mind.
It was Guyue Fang Yuan, his brother!
That C grade talent!
That person who had fallen into depravity after the Awakening Ceremony!
That person who slept soundly in class all day long!
That person who drank too much and didn't return home at night!
That person who bullied Shen Cui, slapped him twice, and took all the Yuan stone he had!
That person who had been suppressing him in the past, like a shadow entrenched in his heart!
"How could this be?
This can't be! "
For a moment, Fangzheng roared in his heart, "I worked so hard, but he drank too much all day long. In the end, he still won first place. Is there any justice left?
Why?
Why?! "
The sun rose from the east. Birds chirped and chirped. Spring swept across Qing Mao Mountain.
Guyue Fangzheng bathed in the warm sunlight. He slowly lowered his head, gritted his teeth, and looked at his lonely shadow on the ground.
The surging emotions in his heart were like a deflated balloon. There wasn't much left.
What replaced them were doubts, resentment, incomprehension, unwillingness, fear, and other complex emotions.
…
As time passed, the sun climbed higher and higher.
A new list was posted on the school's bulletin board. There were only two names on the list. They were Fang Yuan and Fangzheng.
Following the appearance of this list, the news gradually spread.
Those youths who had wholeheartedly stayed at home to refine Gu after receiving their Gu worms were in an uproar after hearing the news.
"How could this be!"
"If it was Fangzheng who got first, I would still believe it. But Fang Yuan, isn't he C grade talent?"
"Are you kidding me? The A grade talent Fangzheng actually lost to the C grade talent Fang Yuan. Is this a fantasy?"
The Mo family.
The small courtyard was full of greenery, and the fragrance of tea wafted in the air.
One of the elders in charge of the Guyue family, Guyue Mochen, sat in front of his desk. He looked at the spring scenery outside the window and drank his tea leisurely. "Hasn't Mobei continued refining the Gu?"
The housekeeper standing at the side quickly replied, "Ever since he heard the news about Fang Yuan in the morning, Young Master Mobei seemed to have suffered a huge blow. He no longer has the mood to continue refining the Moonlight Gu.
Speaking of which, it's a pity that Young Master Mobei was so close to success.
Actually, if Fangzheng got first place, it would be fine. But it just had to be that C grade talent Fang Yuan.
So it's no wonder that Young Master Mobei lost interest. "
"Hmph!
Don't try to excuse him. "
Guyue Mochen snorted coldly. His face was solemn and his tone was steely. "A Gu Master's cultivation is full of hardships. What is this little setback?
That Fang Yuan is only a C grade talent. The reason he got first place was probably due to luck.
The Moonlight Gu he chose had weak willpower, allowing him to beat him to it.
If Mobei can't see through this and can't even handle this setback, then how will he be able to control our Mo family in the future? How will he be able to compete with the Chi family?
No one is allowed to persuade him. Let him think about it carefully! "
"Yes, master." The housekeeper did not dare to refute.
Almost at the same time, in the Chi family.
"Sigh, Guyue Fang Yuan …" The elder Guyue Lian sighed deeply. He frowned and thought for a while. He waved his hand and said, "Someone, call Young Master Chicheng in."
Not long after, Guyue Cheng walked into the room with a face full of disappointment. He knelt down and said, "Grandchild greets Grandpa."
"Looks like you already know about this news."
Guyue Lian stared at his only grandson. His tone was soft and said slowly, "I called you here to prevent you from being affected by this matter.
Refining the natal Gu depends on one's aptitude and the Gu worm. "
"Fang Yuan's aptitude is only C grade, but he was able to get first place. This means that the Gu worm he chose, compared to the Moonlight Gu in your hands, has a much weaker willpower.
This is all luck.
So grandson, don't be discouraged. This is nothing.
He is only C grade. Although he is the same as you, he doesn't have as many resources as you do. His road to promotion will be harder than yours. Believe me, you will soon surpass him. "
"So, let go of this insignificant matter.
Fang Yuan will not be your rival, nor is he worthy of being called your rival.
Your real rival is the A grade Fangzheng, the Mobei of the Mo family.
Do you understand? "
"Yes, thank you grandpa for enlightening me. I understand.
I will go down now and continue refining the Gu! "The disappointment on Guyue Cheng's face disappeared. What replaced it was a spirited fighting spirit.
"Okay."
The elder Guyue Lian nodded his head in satisfaction. He showed a kind and kind smile, "Good grandson, although your true aptitude is only C grade, don't worry, I will support you with all my strength.
Later, I will use the aura of a Rank 3 Gu to suppress the Moonlight Gu's willpower and help you refine this Gu! "

==================================================
S-REV-030.summary: In the Chi family residence, Elder Guyue Lian senses his grandson, Cheng's, disappointment over Fang Yuan's victory. He strategically reassures Cheng, dismissing Fang Yuan's success as luck due to a weak Gu worm and emphasizing that Fangzheng and Mobei are Cheng's true rivals. To further bolster Cheng's confidence and progress, Lian offers to use the aura of a Rank 3 Gu to aid in Cheng's refinement, demonstrating a blend of manipulation and support.

continuityMutations.content: His advanced understanding of Gu Master techniques and cultivation, far beyond his peers, gained from his past life.
His ability to perfectly execute a moonblade attack, which he chooses to conceal from others.
His renewed determination to excel in Gu Master cultivation.
His belief that Fang Yuan's past success was luck, and his resolve to surpass him.
His internal assessment of Gu Master cultivation as 'cruel' due to talent and resource disparities.
His indifference to the Moonlight Gu lesson stems from his past life as a Rank six Gu Master, making the current demonstration seem like child's play.
He deliberately conceals his true proficiency with the Moonlight Gu during practice, only demonstrating a controlled, precise attack when he believes no one is watching.
He calculates his remaining Yuan stones and realizes he will run out in half a month without new income, stressing over the cost of feeding his Gu worms.
He plans to perform well in the upcoming moonblade assessment to win the ten Yuan stone reward.
He buys additional jars of green bamboo wine to stock up before a caravan depletes the local supply, ensuring his Liquor worm has food.
He teaches the youths how to activate and use the Moonlight Gu.
He focuses his individual tutoring on A and B grade talents (Fangzheng, Mobei, Chicheng) while giving only brief advice to C grade students like Fang Yuan, unaware of Fang Yuan's true abilities.
He privately reflects on the 'cruel' nature of Gu Master cultivation, acknowledging the advantages of high talent and wealth for practice.
He feels a renewed determination to excel after seeing an unknown student (mistakenly) perform well with the moonblade.
He believes Fang Yuan's previous success was due to luck and resolves to surpass him in Gu Master cultivation.
The area is specifically designed for Gu Master training.
The bamboo walls sometimes grow wild roses.
Students are provided with basic living arrangements.

S-REV-031.prose:
Chapter 22 The sky was blue and clear, as if it had been washed. The sun was golden. White clouds floated, and a group of colorful parrots chirped, forming an arrow formation under the blue sky and white clouds, flapping their wings and soaring. These colorful parrots only appeared in large groups in spring. Their feathers were colorful, and they were the size of eagles. Their heads and beaks were like parrots, and their tails were like a peacock's, dragging long. It had been more than ten days since Fang Yuan won first place in the test to refine his vital Gu. The spring breeze blew the green grass on the mountain, the wildflowers bloomed, and the bees and butterflies danced around. It was a beautiful scene of spring, full of vitality. The atmosphere of spring was so strong that even the tall bamboo walls around the training field could not cover it. The training field covered an area of three acres. The ground was flat, paved with a thick and wide layer of limestone. The surroundings were transplanted with green spear bamboos. These dark green bamboos were close to each other, straight and straight, forming a circle of tall green walls. Although the corners of the walls were also paved with stone bricks, many places had already sprouted grass. Between the bamboos, there were wild roses that drilled in from the outside, and even climbed up the walls. Fifty-seven fifteen-year-old youths stood in the middle of the training field, forming a semicircle, watching the academy elder in the center. This was a lesson to teach the youths how to use the Moonlight Gu. "The Moonlight Gu is the signature Gu of our Guyue clan, just like the Xiong family's Bear Power Gu and the Bai family's Stream Gu. Most of the people here have chosen the Moonlight Gu as their vital Gu. Watch carefully, I will personally demonstrate how to activate the Moonlight Gu to attack. Students whose vital Gu is not the Moonlight Gu, you must also focus. This classic long-range attack method can be used on other Gu, and has a wide range of applications. " As he spoke, the academy elder stretched out his right hand, spreading his fingers and lowering his palm so that the youths could see his palm. "First, use your mind to move the Moonlight Gu into your own palm." As he spoke, the crescent mark that represented the Moonlight Gu moved along the elder's arm to his palm. "Then, move the primeval essence in your aperture and pour it into the Moonlight Gu." A trace of silver-colored primeval essence gushed out of the elder's body, almost imperceptibly, and entered the Moonlight Gu in his palm. The academy elder was a Rank three Gu Master, and only a Rank three Gu Master could condense a silver luster of primeval essence. A Rank one Gu Master's primeval essence was called bronze primeval essence, while a Rank two Gu Master's was called red steel primeval essence.

Only at the third rank would it be silver primeval essence. After absorbing this sliver of silver true essence, the crescent moon mark in the center of the elder's hand immediately became brighter and brighter. Even though it was daytime, it still emitted a bright pale blue radiance. "Amazing!" "So beautiful." The youths couldn't help but exclaim in amazement when they saw this scene. The pale blue light, as clear as water, flickered faintly in the elder's hand. At first glance, it was as if the academy elder was holding a handful of moonlight in his hand. The academy elder smiled. "Watch carefully. The last step is to shoot it out like I did." As he spoke, he slowly closed his spread fingers, then raised his arm and slowly straightened it. Finally, he waved his palm lightly. His movements were steady and powerful. Whoosh. The youths heard a light sound in their ears. Following the academy elder's movement, the pale blue light condensed on his palm was thrown out just like that. The light formed a small moonblade in the air. The pale blue moonblade was only the size of a palm, just like the crescent moon in the night sky. It drew a straight line in the air, and then hit a grass puppet ten meters away. With a swish, the grass puppet's thirty centimeter thick neck was cut off by the moonblade. The puppet's body swayed, and its huge head fell to the ground. After cutting off the grass puppet, the moonblade immediately dimmed. However, it still flew for another six meters in the air before the crescent moon gradually faded away, finally dissipating into the air. Looking at the grass puppet's neck, the cut surface was extremely smooth, as if it had been cut with the sharpest sickle. Almost all the youths stared with their eyes wide open, looking at this scene in shock. A few of them couldn't help but touch their own necks, shocked by the moonblade's attack power. After a short period of silence, exclamations came again and again. Some of the youths' eyes lit up, staring at the grass puppet. Some stared at the academy elder's palm.

There were also some who whispered to each other, discussing excitedly. Only Fang Yuan was hidden in the crowd, his expression cold and indifferent, not moving at all. In his previous life, his cultivation had reached Rank six, and he founded the Blood Wing Demon Sect in the Central Continent, with tens of thousands of followers. He was known as the Big Shot of the Demonic Path, and his reputation was illustrious. The academy elder was only a Rank three Gu Master, and this method was child's play. It would not cause a ripple in Fang Yuan's heart. "Next, as long as you have successfully refined the Moonlight Gu, step forward. Each of you will have a grass puppet. Follow my method just now, activate the moonblade and practice attacking. " Just as the academy elder finished speaking, more than thirty youths stood up. This year, the entire clan had more than a hundred youths participating in the Awakening Ceremony. There were fifty-seven students who had the talent to cultivate. Among them, thirty-five chose the Moonlight Gu. After these days of hard work, they had all refined the Moonlight Gu. The rest of the youths were all D grade talents. It was not that they did not want to refine the Moonlight Gu, but their talent was not good enough, so they could only retreat. To the Guyue clan youths, the Moonlight Gu was no longer a simple Gu worm, but a symbol of the clan's glory. Soon, thirty-five youths stood in a row. Ten meters away from each of them, there was a grass puppet erected in front of them. Fang Yuan stood in the middle of the line, not attracting attention. Practice began. The youths all stretched out their right hands, transferring the Moonlight Gu into their palms. The blue crescent moon imprints, with the injection of bronze colored primeval essence, emitted aqua blue radiance one by one. But when they cut in the air with their palms, only seven or eight crescent moons flew out. Some of these crescent moons only appeared for a moment, then quickly dissipated. Some just flew out two or three meters, and suddenly with a bang, collapsed into blue flowing light. Some flew far, but the direction was off, directly flying into the sky. The youths all frowned. Watching the elder's demonstration just now, it seemed quite simple. But when they personally tried it out, they realized the knack within.

To send out a moonblade and hit the grass puppet at the same time, it was really not easy. The elder watched with a slight smile. He saw this scene every year, so he was not surprised. The remaining 22 youths could only stand outside the field, watching enviously. After practicing for five minutes, the youths were gradually able to send out the moonblade. For a moment, the sky above the martial arts field was filled with light blue moonblades flying everywhere. Some of the moonblades dissipated halfway, some unfortunately collided with each other, and some flew out of the field, slanted and disorderly. Only a few moonblades were able to hit the grass puppet. Of course, this was a case of a blind cat running into a dead mouse. The academy elder began to conduct individual tutoring. He focused on Fangzheng, Mobei, and Chicheng, these outstanding students, patiently correcting their mistakes in postures and teaching them his own insights. As for C grade students like Fang Yuan, he only gave a few words of advice. Fang Yuan had been condensing the blue light in his hand. He waved his palm a few times in the air, but he condensed it and did not release it. Seeing that the field was chaotic and no one was paying attention to him, he moved his mind and loosened his control of the Moonlight Gu. His palm tilted slightly, making a palm knife slashing action. In order not to attract attention, he did not aim at the grass puppet opposite him, but instead aimed at the one in front of him on the left. With a swoosh, a moonblade flew out quickly, passing through the chaotic area in the center, drawing a straight line in the air, accurately hitting the neck of the grass puppet. The grass puppet trembled, its neck was cut in half by the moonblade, but soon the green grass stalk that was cut began to grow again, tangling together, and the wound healed. This grass puppet, of course, was not an ordinary grass puppet, but a Rank one Grass Puppet Gu. It had a slight self-healing ability of the plant attribute. Unless the grass puppet was cut in half, the wound would heal after a while. "Wow, look at this moonblade!" "Amazing, who sent it?" There were very few moonblades that could hit the grass puppet. Fang Yuan had only casually attacked, but it had created the most obvious attack effect so far. It immediately caused the students outside the field to exclaim in surprise. Even the academy elder's attention was attracted. He asked, "That moonblade just now was not bad. Did you send it?"

He looked at a C grade student with a questioning gaze. Because that puppet corresponded to him. This male student blinked his eyes. Facing the sudden stares of the crowd, he seemed to be at a loss of what to do. To be honest, the scene was too chaotic just now. Moonblades were flying everywhere, and he did not know if he was the one who sent it. "But, it looks like it's possible, right?" The young man thought and nodded subconsciously. The surrounding students suddenly had a whole new level of respect for him. "Who is he? What's his name?" Some of the female students asked the people around them. "Even he can send moonblades, I can't lose!" Guyue Mobei's eyes flashed with determination. "So it wasn't brother who sent it." Guyue Fangzheng let out a sigh of relief. After being comforted by his aunt and uncle, he had already recovered from the previous shock. "Brother, you got first last time because you were lucky and chose a weak-willed Moonlight Gu. A Gu Master's cultivation cannot always rely on luck. I will win against you. "Fangzheng encouraged himself in his heart. "You did well. Continue to work hard and grasp that feeling just now." The academy elder patted the student's shoulder and encouraged him with a smile. The young man nodded his head excitedly, his eyes shining with a different light. The elder took the opportunity to announce, "Listen up, this is your homework. Practice well after class, and we will check the results in three days. Whoever has the best results will get ten Yuan stones as a reward. Do you all hear me? " "Oh!"

The students shouted in response. Hearing that there would be Yuan stones as a reward, they could not help but get even more excited. However, after just three minutes, the moonblades flying in the air gradually became sparse. "Damn it, every moonblade consumes ten percent of my primeval essence." "The consumption of the moonblade is too high. I am only a C grade, and my aperture can only store 38 percent of green copper primeval essence. I can only send out three moonblades in a row. " The youths who stopped all sighed in despair. The academy elder witnessed all this and did not say anything, but he sighed in his heart, "This is the benefit of having a high cultivation talent. To use the moonblade, there are only two words – practice makes perfect. Those with high talent have more primeval essence in their aperture, and their primeval essence recovers faster, giving them more opportunities to practice. Those with low talent can use Yuan stones to make up for it and increase the amount of practice. Those without high talent and without Yuan stones, even if they want to practice, they will not have the strength. Sigh, a Gu Master's cultivation is so cruel. I better take care of those students with high talent. " ================================================== Chapter 23 The sun had set. The sunset glow was still burning in the sky. Looking at the mountains in the distance, they were covered with a thick layer of ash that was slowly turning black. In the academy, the day's lessons had ended. The students walked out in twos and threes. "Today was really a happy day, I learned a lot of things. Especially learning how to use the Moonlight Gu. " "The moonblade flying in the air is so cool. Too bad I don't have enough talent. In the future, I can only be a logistics Gu Master and can't go to the battlefield. "The youths talked with great interest. Some people called their friends. "Let's go eat together and drink some rice wine. How about it?" "Okay, that's a good suggestion."
S-REV-031.summary: The Academy Elder conducts a lesson on the Moonlight Gu, demonstrating its activation and attack for fifty-seven youths. While most students are amazed, Fang Yuan remains indifferent, having been a Rank six Gu Master in his past life. During practice, Fang Yuan secretly executes a perfect moonblade attack, but allows another student to take credit, reigniting Fangzheng's competitive drive against him and solidifying Mobei's determination.

continuityMutations.content: His awareness of Jiang Ya's deceptive pricing strategy for grass puppets.
His attempt to deceive Fang Yuan about the scarcity and price of grass puppets.
He uses scarcity tactics to encourage students to buy grass puppets quickly, inflating the price for profit.
He pretends there are only a few grass puppets left and then reveals an 'eighth one' to Fang Yuan, implying a special favor, despite the actual cost being much lower.
Sells Rank one Gu worms and Gu-related supplies.
Grass puppets are a common training tool.

C-REV-01.transition: Walked alone to the shop after lessons.

S-REV-032.prose:
"You guys go first. I have to go to the shop near the academy's Gu room to buy a grass puppet so I can practice at home." … Fang Yuan came to the Gu room alone. The academy's Gu room stored many Rank one Gu worms of various types. Fang Yuan's Moonlight Gu was chosen from there for free. Every once in a while, the students would have a chance to get a Gu worm for free. If they wanted to get additional Gu worms, they had to pay. Fang Yuan did not have the intention to refine other Gu worms in the short term. He walked to the shop next to the Gu room. It was a small shop. There were seven students in the shop, bargaining with the shop owner for grass puppets. "It's junior brother." The Rank one Gu Master in charge of the shop was in his twenties. After seeing Fang Yuan, he did his business and took the initiative to greet Fang Yuan. Fang Yuan was surprised. This Gu Master was Jiang Ya. The young Gu Master who taught the hunters in the inn a lesson. "Oh, it's senior." Fang Yuan nodded, his face expressionless. Jiang Ya took out a grass puppet from the counter behind him and handed it to a student who was buying it. He smiled at Fang Yuan in a friendly manner and asked, "Junior, are you here to buy grass puppets too? Do I need to save one for you? Only three Yuan stone. This thing sells very fast. Now there are only seven left. If you wait any longer, there will be no more stock. " He had an arrogant attitude towards mortals, but towards Fang Yuan and the others, he was very friendly. Fang Yuan shook his head, laughing in his heart. This Jiang Ya really knows how to do business. The grass puppet was made from the Grass Man Gu.

Including the input of primeval essence, the cost was only one and a half Yuan stone. "Senior, that's not very nice of you. First come, first served. Why should we leave it for him? " "That's right, we've been here for a long time. You have to follow the rules when doing business. " "Three pieces it is. You can have the Yuan stone and I'll have the puppet." When the youths in the shop heard that there were only seven puppets left, they became anxious. They stopped bargaining and took out their Yuan stone to buy the puppets. Soon, the seven of them left with satisfaction. "Junior Brother, do you want to buy a grass puppet?" Jiang Ya asked with a smile. "They said that they were just sold out, but there is actually an eighth one. If Junior Brother doesn't buy it, then you'll miss a good opportunity. " Fang Yuan had no interest in the grass puppet. He shook his head and took out a Yuan stone, putting it on the counter. "I want to buy ten moon orchid petals." Jiang Ya stared blankly at Fang Yuan. He took away the Yuan stone and pulled open the drawer of the counter. He took out a paper bag. "Ten moon orchid petals, not one less. You can count them." Fang Yuan checked it in person. Finding that there were no mistakes, he left the small shop. Gu worms need to be fed. Gu Masters refine and use Gu, and at the same time, they also need to raise Gu. Refining Gu is difficult, and there is the danger of backlash. Using Gu is not easy, and requires a lot of practice. The knowledge of raising Gu is even more extensive and profound.
S-REV-032.summary: After the lesson, Fang Yuan visits the Gu Room Shop to buy moon orchid petals for his Moonlight Gu. He encounters Jiang Ya, the shop owner, who attempts to use deceptive sales tactics to sell him a grass puppet at an inflated price. Fang Yuan, having discovered Jiang Ya's inflated pricing, declines the grass puppet but purchases the moon orchid petals, calculating his immediate expenses.

continuityMutations.content: His precise calculation of Gu feeding costs and his dwindling financial reserves.
His strategy to buy green bamboo wine in bulk due to market scarcity, demonstrating foresightedness.
His knowledge about the upcoming caravan and its impact on the green bamboo wine supply.
He informs Fang Yuan about an upcoming caravan purchase that will deplete the inn's green bamboo wine stock.
A regular source of green bamboo wine, a key ingredient for feeding certain Gu worms.
Caravans periodically visit to purchase local goods like green bamboo wine.

C-REV-01.transition: Walked from the inn to the academy dormitory, accompanied by shop assistants carrying wine jars.

S-REV-033.prose:
Because there are all kinds of Gu worms, their food is also different. Some need to swallow soil, some need starlight, some need to drink tears, and some need to absorb the clouds of the nine heavens. Take the three Gu worms that Fang Yuan had now for example. The Moonlight Gu needs to swallow moon orchid petals twice a day, one in the morning and one in the evening, and each meal has two petals. As for the Liquor worm, it needs to drink wine. A jar of green bamboo wine can support it for four days. The Spring Autumn Cicada is even more peculiar. It can directly drink water from the River of Time to maintain its vitality. The River of Time supports the operation of this world. It is not far away in the sky, but close at hand, flowing by everyone's side. Every action of all living beings requires the impetus of time. Time is like flowing water, passing by quickly. The River of Time is invisible and colorless, and all living beings actually live and exist in the river of time. After buying this bag of moon orchid petals, Fang Yuan went to the inn to buy green bamboo wine. The Liquor worm can also drink some wine and rice wine to survive. But once it is this kind of inferior wine, the amount it drinks is huge, and it needs a few jars every day. Fang Yuan calculated, it is better to buy green bamboo wine directly. Not only is it more cost-effective than buying inferior wine, it will also not arouse suspicion. "Young master, you are here." The inn's waiter already knew Fang Yuan. Fang Yuan directly threw three pieces of Yuan stone to him, and said with familiarity, "Give me a jar of green bamboo wine, and give me a few good dishes. No need for change, put it here first, and at the end of the month we will settle it all together, any overpayment will be refunded, any shortfall will be made up. " Although he no longer lives in the inn, but moved to the school dormitory. But every time he buys wine, he will eat here. "Okay. Young master, please sit here, the wine and dishes will be ready soon. "The waiter responded and led Fang Yuan to a seat. He took out the rag on his shoulder, politely wiped the table and stools, and then left. As the waiter said, the wine and dishes were quickly served. Fang Yuan ate while calculating in his heart, "One Yuan stone can buy ten petals, and the Moonlight Gu consumes four every day.

One jar of green bamboo wine is worth two Yuan stone, and can support the Liquor worm for four days. That is to say, just feeding these two Gu will consume nearly one Yuan stone every day. " This may seem small, but it is actually very expensive. For a mortal family of three, a month's living expenses are only one Yuan stone. Since refining the Gu until now, it has been sixteen days. Just raising the Gu cost Fang Yuan 14.5 Yuan stones. "I got the Flower Wine Inheritance, took away Fangzheng's bag of Yuan stones, and got the first prize. My Yuan stone assets were as high as 44.5 Yuan stones. But refining the Gu used 6.5 Yuan stones, feeding it took 14.5 Yuan stones, and half a Yuan stone for living expenses. Now I should be left with 20 Yuan stones. " Fang Yuan took out his money bag and opened it. The bag was filled with pieces of Yuan stone. These Yuan stones were grayish-white in color, each oval, the same volume, and the size of a duck's egg. Counting, there were really only 20 Yuan stones left. That is to say, without any income, the Yuan stone Fang Yuan had left was only enough for him to last for half a month. He was not like other people of the same age, who more or less had friends and relatives to help them. Especially students like Guyue Mobei and Guyue Cheng, there was no shortage of Yuan stone. And Fang Yuan could only think of a way himself. "Uncle and Aunt have already cut off my living expenses, but every weekend, the family academy will give out three Yuan stone as a subsidy to the students. Looks like I should perform well in the moonblade assessment three days later, and get the ten Yuan stone as a reward. "Fang Yuan chewed on his food as he thought. At his age, his body was growing. Unknowingly, all the food had fallen into his stomach. Picking up the unopened green bamboo wine, Fang Yuan lifted his feet and walked out of the inn. "Young master, young master." The shop assistant behind him chased after him, "Let me tell young master something, in less than a month, there will be a caravan coming to the village.

According to the usual practice, they will buy our green bamboo wine. Young master loves green bamboo wine, and will buy a few jars every week. The shopkeeper instructed me to tell young master about this. Our green bamboo wine is limited, and after selling it to the caravan, I'm afraid there won't be much left. " "Is that so?" Fang Yuan heard this and frowned slightly. Fang Yuan had five hundred years of experience in reading people and deciphering words. The shop assistant and the young Gu Master Jiang Ya said similar things, but Fang Yuan could naturally tell that Jiang Ya was lying, and the shop assistant was telling the truth. This was a bit troublesome. Fang Yuan needed to feed the Liquor worm, and in the long run, the amount of green bamboo wine needed was very large. If this inn was out of stock, he was afraid that in the future he would have to use a lot of inferior wine to feed the Liquor worm. He could not drink several jars of wine in a day, and after a long time, it would arouse suspicion. After thinking for a while, Fang Yuan took out ten Yuan stone, "Then buy another five jars and let me take them. Come with me to the academy dormitory." "Yes, young master." The shop assistant quickly took the Yuan stone. If there was no storage method, the moon orchid petals could only be stored for five days, so Fang Yuan only bought one bag at a time. But green bamboo wine could be stored for a long time, so there was no problem in this aspect. A few shop assistants followed Fang Yuan into the academy dormitory, placed the wine jars under the bed, and left. Looking at the suddenly deflated money bag in his hand, Fang Yuan sighed. Refining Gu was hard, and raising Gu was not easy. This was because he had five hundred years of experience from his previous life. He did not need to practice using Gu worms, which meant that the consumption of primeval essence was reduced, and this saved him a lot of expenses. Like the people around him, to practice using the Moonlight Gu, they had to consume primeval essence. To increase proficiency, they had to practice a few times. The consumption of primeval essence was too high, and the recovery was too slow, so they could only use Yuan stone to replenish. Buying a grass puppet would cost three primeval stones. This was all money. "Luckily my Spring Autumn Cicada consumes time, and not other food. Otherwise, I would have gone bankrupt long ago, and could not afford it.

"Fang Yuan felt very lucky. The higher the level of Gu worm, the larger the appetite, or the rarer the food, the harder it was to raise. A Rank two ordinary Gu worm would consume one to two primeval stones a day. It was still good to be able to buy food. Some Gu worm's food was especially hard to find, and the market simply did not have such goods. Just like the Spring Autumn Cicada's food was time, which was even more precious. As the saying goes, an inch of gold cannot buy an inch of time. No matter how much money you have, can you buy time? You can't! In theory, there was no limit to the number of Gu a Gu Master could refine. As long as you could refine, ten, a hundred, or even a thousand would do. You could refine however many Gu you wanted to refine. But in fact, a Gu Master usually only had four or five Gu worms. Why? The biggest reason was that they could not afford to raise them. The higher the rank of the Gu worm, the more expensive it was to feed it. This often caused Gu Masters to be strapped for cash, giving them a headache. Another reason was that they could not afford to use it. Activating the Moonlight Gu, one moonblade attack would consume 10% of primeval essence. A C grade Gu Master could only launch three to four attacks, and the primeval essence in his aperture would be used up. No matter how many Gu worms you raised, if you could not use them, wouldn't it all be a waste? Thus, there was a saying in Gu Master cultivation. Raising Gu was like raising a mistress. To raise a mistress, one had to buy food, clothes, a house and so on. It was very expensive, and raising too many would cost too much. Normal people could not afford it. Even if they could raise so many, a man's energy was limited, and he could not afford it. Was he going to raise it for nothing? The Gu Master's realm increased, but the Gu worm's food standard also increased. Thus, don't think that Gu Masters had no limit to the number of Gu they could refine.

Most Gu Masters would only raise four or five Gu worms of the same rank. If the number was any higher, the Gu Master would go bankrupt!
S-REV-033.summary: Fang Yuan visits the inn to purchase green bamboo wine for his Liquor worm, reflecting on the high daily cost of feeding his Gu worms. He calculates his remaining Yuan stones, realizing his precarious financial situation. The inn waiter informs him of an upcoming caravan that will deplete the green bamboo wine stock, prompting Fang Yuan to immediately buy five extra jars to secure his supply, further straining his finances.

continuityMutations.content: The Academy Elder initially dismisses Fang Yuan due to his C-grade talent and past 'luck,' and plans to favor Fangzheng for first place due to clan politics.
Fangzheng believes he has finally surpassed Fang Yuan after Fang Yuan's initial 'miss' during the moonblade test, feeling a surge of triumph and hope to remove his brother's shadow from his life.
Fang Yuan deliberately missed his first moonblade attack to set up a later, more impactful display of skill and to manage expectations.

Fang Yuan believes that 'glory' and 'power' are tools used by superiors to exploit subordinates, and that resources (like Yuan stones) are the true foundation of strength, leading him to rob his classmates.
Guyue Mobei sees Guyue Cheng and Guyue Fangzheng as his main rivals for academic superiority.
Guyue Cheng views Guyue Mobei and Fang Yuan as rivals, and is suspicious of Fang Yuan's success.
Guyue Cheng's true talent is C grade, and his B grade appearance is due to cheating.
The Academy Elder is aware of the political struggles within the clan and tries to remain neutral, but leans towards supporting the clan leader's side, which influences his grading decisions.
The Academy Elder understands how to motivate students using incentives like status (class monitor) and rewards (Yuan stones).
Guyue Fangzheng is deeply motivated to surpass Fang Yuan, especially after feeling cheated out of first place in a previous assessment due to Fang Yuan's 'luck'.
Fangzheng's confidence is severely shaken after Fang Yuan's unexpected performance in the moonblade test.
The place where basic combat techniques and Gu usage are taught.
Equipped with various training puppets for students to practice on.

addedNodes.concept: Gu worm utility vs. basic martial arts

S-REV-034.prose:
Chapter 24
Three days later.
"Lowering your body to dodge is a common technique to restrain swinging punches.
When the enemy attacks, quickly crouch down and take the opportunity to counterattack, attacking his crotch and stomach.
Don't be afraid of swinging punches. Those who often use swinging punches are brainless and impulsive people. "
On the training field, the academy's boxing instructor spoke as he demonstrated the moves.
A wooden puppet swept its right fist over. The boxing instructor crouched down and dodged the attack, then punched the puppet's stomach. After a few punches, the wooden puppet fell to the ground.
The students gathered in a circle to watch, but most of them could not raise their spirits, showing a lack of interest.
The academy taught many kinds of courses, and this class was teaching the basics of boxing.
Using fists and legs to exert strength was much inferior to the cool and cool moonblade attacks. The youths were almost distracted.
"The next class is the Moonlight Gu's usage test.
How's your training recently? "
"Not bad, I can use all three moonblades, but rarely can I hit all of them.
Usually, there are two hits on the grass puppet. "
"Hmm, about the same as me.
These days, in order to practice this, I bought a grass puppet. "
…
The youths were whispering, their minds were no longer here, they were all worried about the next class's test.
For this test, they had trained hard for a long time after class. Now they were all full of energy, looking forward to the test.
The students' discussion entered the instructor's ears. The boxing instructor turned around and shouted, "Talking is forbidden in class, all of you shut up and watch carefully!"
He was a Rank two Gu Master, his body was muscular, his upper body was strong, and his bronze skin was covered with scars.
His shout was majestic, immediately suppressing all the youths in the field.
The Martial Arts Practice Field was silent.
"The basics of boxing is the most important.
Especially in the early stages of a Gu Master's cultivation, it is more important than anything else.
All of you focus! "
After a round of reprimanding, the martial arts instructor summoned another wooden puppet.
This light yellow wooden puppet was two meters tall, and when its wooden feet stepped on the bluestone floor, it made a crisp sound.
It opened its arms and clumsily rushed towards the instructor.
The instructor dodged its attack, then suddenly hugged its waist and pushed it forward, pushing the wooden puppet to the ground.
Then, he rode on the wooden puppet's waist and quickly punched the wooden puppet's head.
The wooden puppet resisted for a moment before its head was smashed by the instructor's rain-like fists. It lay paralyzed on the ground, motionless.
The martial arts instructor stood up. His breath was still calm and long. He explained to the students, "Don't be afraid when facing a tall enemy in close combat.
Destroying the opponent's center of gravity is a wise tactic to control the enemy. Just like what I did just now, I grabbed the enemy's waist, controlled his hip bone, and pushed him forward.
After that, he would ride on the opponent's body and punch them fiercely. Those who have no intention of defending will instantly collapse. "
The students nodded repeatedly, but most of their gazes were filled with disapproval.
The head instructor saw everything and smiled bitterly in his heart.
It had always been like this in the past. Young people were naturally easily attracted by gorgeous things.
Without personal experience, it was difficult for the youngsters to understand the importance of basic punches and kicks.
In fact, especially to early stage Gu Masters, basic punches and kicks may seem inconspicuous, but they are more important than moonblade attacks.
"… Remember, in close combat, don't just stare at the enemy's eyes, but pay attention to their shoulders.
Whether the enemy punches or kicks, their shoulders will move first … "
"… Speed is very important in close combat. The speed here is not the speed of the attack, but the speed of the feet.
… "
"… Distance is the best defense …"
"… Legs need to be flexible, that way it will be easier to explode with power …"
"… When moving and punching, maintain a triangular support.
Otherwise, if you lose control of your lower body, the enemy will not fall, but you will fall instead … "
The martial arts instructor explained patiently as he demonstrated.
This was the precious experience he had accumulated through long periods of battles, using blood and tears.
It was a pity that the students outside did not realize this.
They gradually started to whisper to each other again, the focus of their discussion was still on the moonblade assessment in the next lesson.
"This martial arts instructor is very practical, but there's a problem with his teaching method." Fang Yuan watched quietly from the crowd, nodding and shaking his head.
This instructor's teaching was not methodical, it was purely out of interest, teaching whatever he wanted to teach.
Thus, the things he taught were messy, many and complicated. Many students listened attentively at first, but gradually lost interest and shifted their attention to other aspects.
Only Fang Yuan listened attentively. Others were learning, but he was revising.
His battle experience was much richer than the martial arts instructor's, but listening to someone else's explanation was also a form of cultivation verification.
Gu Master's fighting style was usually divided into close combat and long range.
The moonblade attack was the long range type, but strictly speaking, it could only be considered mid to long range.
