Fangzheng believes he has finally surpassed Fang Yuan after Fang Yuan's initial 'miss' during the moonblade test, feeling a surge of triumph and hope to remove his brother's shadow from his life.
Guyue Fangzheng is deeply motivated to surpass Fang Yuan, especially after feeling cheated out of first place in a previous assessment due to Fang Yuan's 'luck'.
Fangzheng's confidence is severely shaken after Fang Yuan's unexpected performance in the moonblade test.
Fangzheng is motivated to win the class monitor position to finally surpass Fang Yuan and make his brother 'bow and greet' him.
His shock and disapproval of Fang Yuan's actions, but eventual forced compliance.
Fangzheng is deeply shocked and disapproves of Fang Yuan's violent extortion of classmates, including himself.
Fangzheng is compelled by Fang Yuan's threat and the situation to comply with the extortion.
His internal debate about whether to plead for Fang Yuan, highlighting his moral conflict.
Fangzheng is aware of Fang Yuan blocking the academy door and robbing students, and the negative influence this caused.
Fangzheng struggles internally on whether to plead for his brother Fang Yuan, weighing family ties against Fang Yuan's perceived misdeeds.
Guyue Fangzheng is short on Yuan stones due to Fang Yuan taking his allowance and his aunt's financial difficulties.
Guyue Fangzheng is determined to surpass Fang Yuan in cultivation due to his A-grade talent.
Guyue Fangzheng is close to Rank one middle stage, expecting to advance in three to four days.
He is struggling with limited Yuan stones, affecting his cultivation pace.
He believes his A-grade talent guarantees he will advance faster than Fang Yuan and eventually surpass him.
Fang Yuan's advancement deeply shocked him and shattered his self-confidence.
His confidence was severely impacted by Fang Yuan's unexpected advancement.
He feels further overshadowed and demoralized by his brother's actions and his own secondary position.
He is demotivated by Fang Yuan's early advancement and the subsequent events, feeling overshadowed despite his own progress.
He is appointed a vice-class monitor but finds the position undermined and is still extorted by Fang Yuan, feeling powerless against his brother.
He is forced to pay Fang Yuan despite his new position, highlighting his powerlessness against his brother.
Is identified by Guyue Bo as a 'unpolished gem' to be trained through exposure to Fang Yuan's challenges.
Will receive personal, secret guidance from Clan Leader Guyue Bo.
He is secretly receiving preferential instruction from Guyue Bo.
He is determined to surpass Fang Yuan.
He is receiving secret, preferential instruction from clan leader Guyue Bo.
He is determined to surpass his older brother, Fang Yuan.
Chooses a Bronze Skin Gu for defense.
Is manipulated by Guyue Bo into striving to be the first to reach Rank Two in exchange for a Jade Skin Gu.
Learns that clan leaders assess character and potential from seemingly simple choices like Gu worm selection.
Guyue Bo recognizes his low self-esteem and uses it to motivate him.
His acquisition of the Bronze Skin Gu for defense.
His newfound understanding of how Gu worm choices reveal character and potential, as taught by Guyue Bo.
His commitment to becoming the first Rank Two Gu Master to earn the Jade Skin Gu and overcome his insecurity.
Is the first student to advance to upper stage cultivation.
Believes he has finally surpassed his brother, Fang Yuan.
Students are rewarded with Yuan stones for advancement.
Mid-year and end-of-year assessments are held to temper students.
He was the target of Wang Da's assassination attempt, mistaken for Fang Yuan.
He instinctively used Jade Skin Gu to defend against Wang Da's attack, but was still poisoned by Love Separation.
Fangzheng becomes the accidental target of Wang Da's assassination attempt, mistaken for Fang Yuan.
Fangzheng instinctively activates Jade Skin Gu under extreme duress, saving his life but still suffering from Love Separation poison.
Fangzheng is left in a state of shock and unconsciousness after the assassination attempt.
Guyue Fangzheng is the subject of clan political maneuvering due to his A-grade talent, with Guyue Bo taking him under his personal tutelage.
Achieved Rank two initial stage, surpassing all his peers, including Fang Yuan, Mobei, and Chicheng.
Excitedly believes he has surpassed Fang Yuan, feeling a new sense of confidence and independence from his brother's shadow.
Received a Jade Skin Gu from clan leader Guyue Bo, implying special favor and support for his advancement.
Transformed into an extremely hardworking and arduous cultivator after a poisoning incident, becoming the first to reach Rank one upper stage and then Rank one peak stage among his peers.
He believes primeval essence level determines victory.
He desires to defeat Fang Yuan to shatter the 'shadow' in his heart and achieve rebirth.
He has learned and applies the clan leader's battle experience, including specific dodging movements and counter-attacks.
The primary venue for the year-end exam combat trials.
He knows Fang Yuan's primeval essence is green copper and at 44% capacity.
Fang Yuan's presence and actions inflict a deep psychological shadow, causing him to panic and forget his abilities.
He suffers severe physical injuries, including a bruised scalp, blood loss, and a nearly pierced palm, with his Moonlight Gu severely damaged.
Fangzheng possesses the Jade Skin Gu, a defensive Gu worm he had kept secret.
Fangzheng gains invisible strength and resolve from the crowd's cheers and past memories of support.
Fangzheng reveals his trump card, the Jade Skin Gu, in response to Fang Yuan's pressure.
Fangzheng suffers a severe psychological blow by being defeated after activating his hidden power and building up immense confidence.
The shame and embarrassment he feels due to his brother's actions and public perception.
Felt ashamed of Fang Yuan's reputation and actions.
The principles of Gu refinement, including single refinement, fusion, and the importance of recipes.
The existence of Rank five Gu recipes, specifically for a 'Moonlight Treasure King Gu'.
The difficulty and risk of Gu fusion, including the need for consciousness manipulation and the potential for Gu worms to be damaged or die from failure.
He attempts Gu fusion for the first time, learning about its difficulty and requirements.
He learns about Rank five Gu recipes and the potential to refine a 'Moonlight Treasure King Gu'.
He experiences the difficulty and risk of Gu refinement firsthand after a failed attempt.
She is a spy for Fang Yuan's aunt and uncle, and her mother is the powerful housekeeper, Shen momo.
She attempts to gain Fang Yuan's favor and become his concubine.
Fang Yuan's personal maidservant, also acting as a spy for his aunt and uncle.
Hopes to elevate her status by becoming Fang Yuan's concubine due to his perceived talent.
Shen Cui realizes Fang Yuan is aware of the framing plot and is a dangerous, ruthless individual.
Shen Cui is used by Uncle and Aunt Guyue to seduce and frame Fang Yuan.
Shen Cui is the daughter of Shen Mama and serves as Fang Yuan's personal maid.
Is 'given' to Fangzheng as his personal maid.
Her emotional distress is used by Fangzheng to confront Fang Yuan, and she is 'sold' to Fangzheng.
Is the focus of Fangzheng's romantic longing and guilt.
Oversees the Awakening Ceremony and calls out the names of youths for the aptitude test.
Shows concern and disappointment over low aptitude results and joy over high ones, reflecting his dedication to the clan's future.
His deep disappointment in Fang Yuan's C-grade talent and continued 'dispirited' behavior, deeming him a waste of clan resources.
Is responsible for teaching the new Gu Masters about their cultivation path and Gu types.
Is disappointed in Fang Yuan's C-grade talent and lack of discipline, viewing him as a waste of clan resources.
Recounts clan history, praising past leaders and demonizing the Flower Wine Monk.
He learns that Fang Yuan, a C-grade talent, has achieved first place in Moonlight Gu refinement, defying all his expectations.
He initially assumes Fangzheng, an A-grade talent, is the first to refine a Moonlight Gu, reflecting a strong bias towards talent over effort.
He is utterly astonished and perplexed by Fang Yuan's C-grade talent achieving first place.
His internal assessment of Gu Master cultivation as 'cruel' due to talent and resource disparities.
He teaches the youths how to activate and use the Moonlight Gu.
He focuses his individual tutoring on A and B grade talents (Fangzheng, Mobei, Chicheng) while giving only brief advice to C grade students like Fang Yuan, unaware of Fang Yuan's true abilities.
He privately reflects on the 'cruel' nature of Gu Master cultivation, acknowledging the advantages of high talent and wealth for practice.
The Academy Elder initially dismisses Fang Yuan due to his C-grade talent and past 'luck,' and plans to favor Fangzheng for first place due to clan politics.
The Academy Elder is aware of the political struggles within the clan and tries to remain neutral, but leans towards supporting the clan leader's side, which influences his grading decisions.
The Academy Elder understands how to motivate students using incentives like status (class monitor) and rewards (Yuan stones).
Recognition of Fang Yuan's battle talent despite his C-grade aptitude.
The philosophy that controlled fighting fosters student development and fighting spirit.
The inherent power dynamic between Guyue clan members and mortal guards.
His specific threshold for acceptable extortion (one Yuan stone).
The Academy Elder recognizes Fang Yuan's natural talent for battle and strategic thinking, despite his C-grade aptitude.
The Academy Elder understands the inherent power dynamic between Guyue clan members (Gu Masters) and mortal guards, and uses it to assert authority.
The Academy Elder uses a 'stick and carrot' approach to control his guards, removing one and offering promotion to others.
The Academy Elder believes controlled fighting among students is beneficial for tempering their fighting spirit, rather than detrimental.
The Academy Elder sets a specific threshold for Fang Yuan's extortion – one Yuan stone is acceptable, two would warrant intervention.
His understanding that Fang Yuan's actions are beneficial for student development and his strategic inaction.
The Academy Elder understands that Fang Yuan's actions are beneficial for student development and chooses to 'close one eye', actively encouraging the conflict as a training method.
Fang Yuan killed Gao Wan in cold blood and dismembered his body.
Fang Yuan delivered Gao Wan's dismembered body to the Mo family's back door.
Fang Yuan is a 'battle seed' with exceptional combat talent and ruthlessness.
He encourages fights among students for growth but tries to suppress Fang Yuan's overwhelming limelight.
He is deeply disturbed by Fang Yuan's cold indifference and dismemberment of Gao Wan's body.
He struggles with how to handle the political fallout of Fang Yuan killing a Mo family servant within the academy and provoking the Mo family.
He admires Fang Yuan's battle talent and ruthlessness, seeing him as a 'battle seed'.
He instructs the instructor to suppress Fang Yuan's influence to prevent other students from losing courage.
Fang Yuan's independence and lack of fear challenge the clan's traditional methods of control.
The emergence of social circles around Fangzheng, Chicheng, and Mobei presents an opportunity to exert control and suppress Fang Yuan.
The clan uses a long-term strategy of 'brainwashing,' rewards, and systemic integration to control its members, even the rebellious ones.
Fang Yuan's rejection of other youths who tried to rely on him because he considered them useless.
The Academy Elder feels an unusual disgust towards Fang Yuan, which clashes with his motto of teaching without discrimination, leading him to question his own composure.
The Academy Elder believes Fang Yuan's refusal to revere the clan or his teachers makes him a threat to the clan's control and influence over other students, necessitating his suppression.
The Academy Elder identifies Guyue Fangzheng, Chicheng, and Mobei as the most likely candidates to advance to Rank one middle stage first, hoping they can suppress Fang Yuan's influence.
The Academy Elder understands the clan's system of 'brainwashing' and resource control to integrate and control its members, believing it will eventually bind even Fang Yuan.
A place for nurturing battle instincts and identifying future leaders
Social circles form naturally among students, indicating nascent power structures
Contains the Academy Elder's study table with his motto: 'As a teacher, teach without discrimination'
Fang Yuan has truly advanced to Rank one middle stage, despite his C-grade talent.
Fang Yuan's explanation for his advancement ('Heaven rewards the diligent') is suspicious.
He cannot punish Fang Yuan for his actions against the guards due to clan rules and Fang Yuan's 'outstanding' achievement.
Fang Yuan is highly cunning and manipulative, seeing through the elder's attempts to suppress him.
His authority was directly challenged and undermined by Fang Yuan's actions and public performance.
Forced to acknowledge Fang Yuan's advancement and unable to punish him due to clan rules and public perception, despite his suspicions.
Felt publicly shamed and outmaneuvered by Fang Yuan, likening it to Fang Yuan 'pooping on his face'.
The primary institution for nurturing new Gu Masters for the clan.
He realizes Fang Yuan's rejection of the class monitor position is a refusal to be integrated into the family system, deepening his frustration.
He is perceived by the clan as potentially derelict for not understanding Fang Yuan's advancement, pressuring him to provide an explanation.
He attempts to integrate Fang Yuan into the clan system by offering him the class monitor position, hoping to control his behavior and uncover his secrets.
He becomes enraged and frustrated by Fang Yuan's open rejection of the system and continued predatory actions, which undermine his authority and the clan's structure.
He realizes that intervening directly against Fang Yuan would lead to political pressure from the clan leader and other influential families, limiting his options.
He is actively investigating Fang Yuan's background and recent activities to understand his secret advancement, finding his life meticulously simple but yielding no answers.
The place where young Gu Masters are trained, and where clan hierarchy and power struggles manifest among the youth.
He understands that his authority is constrained by the larger political interests of the Guyue clan's leadership.
He learns that Jia Fu has returned and Jia Jin Sheng is missing, an urgent clan matter.
A place where the Academy Elder strategizes and deals with sensitive clan matters, often under pressure from higher authorities.
The implications of Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance are dire for Guyue village's trade and reputation.
Jia Fu is physically present and asserting dominance over the Guyue clan leadership.
Is responsible for the academy and its students, including Guyue Fang Yuan.
Understands the severe implications of Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance for Guyue village and their trade relations.
Initially views Fang Yuan's stubbornness as a clear sign of guilt, then reinterprets it as a facade to hide the Liquor worm, and his innocence regarding Jia Jin Sheng's murder.
The primary meeting place for the Guyue clan's leadership.
Historically founded by Rank five experts, known for its resilience and protective leaders.
Led by a Rank five Gu Master, known for having powerful Rank four Gu Masters like Jia Fu and Jia Gui competing for leadership.
Guyue Bo suspects Fang Yuan is involved in Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance.
Fang Yuan's stubbornness was a facade, and he is not the meticulous murderer, but rather a victim of circumstance.
Guyue Bo supports protecting Fang Yuan from external accusations.
Follows Guyue Bo's implicit instruction to protect Fang Yuan, even when it means contradicting Jia Fu.
Initially misinterprets Guyue Bo's Ren Zu story as a directive to cease suppressing Fang Yuan, rather than a broader lesson on clan governance.
Worries that Fang Yuan's aggressive behavior will demoralize other students.
Understands Guyue Bo's final message about the importance of 'blood ties' and Fang Yuan's connection to Guyue Fangzheng.
Guyue Bo's strategy is to use Fang Yuan's actions to toughen other clan youths, rather than suppressing him.
Guyue Bo believes 'blood ties' and magnanimity are more important than rigid rules in clan governance.
Guyue Bo will personally mentor Guyue Fangzheng.
Fang Yuan is Guyue Fangzheng's twin brother, which makes him politically significant to the clan.
He understands that Guyue Bo is secretly mentoring Guyue Fangzheng.
He has adopted a 'laissez-faire' attitude towards Fang Yuan due to clan leader Guyue Bo's intervention.
Announces the mid-year assessment details.
Believes Fang Yuan's decision not to extort key students signifies him 'lowering his head' and integrating into the family system.
Orders close attention to the safety of Fangzheng, Chicheng, and Mobei during the assessment.
Believes Fang Yuan's recent actions indicate he is 'lowering his head' to the family system.
Is tracking student performance in the assessment and is surprised by Fang Yuan's lead.
Fangzheng was carried away by clan elders after an assassination attempt.
The Academy Elder is responsible for the students' assessment and safety, expressing concern over Fangzheng's assassination.
The Academy Elder suspects the Bai or Xiong clans are behind Fangzheng's assassination, linking it to inter-clan rivalry.
Fang Yuan has likely been recruited by one of the powerful clan elders and is beginning to integrate into the family system.
The Academy Elder observes the clan's political dealings regarding Fang Yuan, believing Fang Yuan has been recruited and is integrating into the family system.
The Academy Elder accepts Guyue Bo's order to pass a message and reward to Fang Yuan.
Oversees the academy's year-end examinations and announces important information regarding rewards and future opportunities.
The Academy Elder suspects Fang Yuan's strength is abnormal and tries to find hidden Gu worms.
The Academy Elder uses the pretense of healing to inspect Fang Yuan's aperture for hidden Gu worms.
The Academy Elder's suspicions about Fang Yuan's strength and lack of visible Gu worms remain, despite finding nothing.
Revealed to have no Gu Master aptitude after taking only three steps in the test.
Revealed to have no Gu Master aptitude after taking four steps in the test.
Achieves B-grade aptitude, taking 36 steps, and has a 66% primeval sea.
Identified as the grandson of Guyue Mo Chen, one of the clan elders.
He has lost motivation to refine his Gu after hearing of Fang Yuan's success.
He loses the will to continue refining his Gu after hearing of Fang Yuan's first-place achievement.
His renewed determination to excel in Gu Master cultivation.
He feels a renewed determination to excel after seeing an unknown student (mistakenly) perform well with the moonblade.
Guyue Mobei sees Guyue Cheng and Guyue Fangzheng as his main rivals for academic superiority.
Guyue Mobei is quickly defeated and rendered unconscious by Fang Yuan's precise attack to his collarbone.
His increased motivation for training due to humiliation by Fang Yuan.
Guyue Mobei feels significant shame and anger after being defeated twice by Fang Yuan.
Guyue Mobei's defeats at Fang Yuan's hands spur him to train harder in martial arts.
His attempt to hide the shameful truth of his defeats from his sister.
Fang Yuan killed Gao Wan, a Mo family servant, and dismembered his body.
Fang Yuan is a dangerous and ruthless killer.
He is deeply shocked, worried, and afraid after learning Fang Yuan killed Gao Wan, someone he knew.
He was present when Mo Yan brought servants, including Gao Wan, to confront Fang Yuan.
Has improved fist and leg skills due to being extorted by Fang Yuan.
He frequently challenges Fang Yuan in the academy arena and is repeatedly defeated.
He is kicked in the stomach by Fang Yuan, causing him to vomit blood, marking a more severe injury than usual.
Guyue Mobei has sustained internal injuries but has recovered.
Guyue Mobei noticed Fang Yuan's hurried breathing during their fight, concluding Fang Yuan is not as strong as generally perceived.
Guyue Mobei receives encouragement and care from his sister, Mo Yan.
Guyue Mobei plans to use his recovery time to nourish his aperture walls and advance to middle stage, aiming to become class monitor and suppress Fang Yuan.
Guyue Mobei is determined to defeat Fang Yuan in a fair fight and prove the importance of talent in cultivation.
Fang Yuan showed a moment of weakness (hurried breathing) during their fight.
He plans to use his recovery time to advance to Rank one middle stage and secure the class monitor position to suppress Fang Yuan.
He is frustrated and disbelieving of Fang Yuan's rapid advancement.
Expressed frustration and disbelief at Fang Yuan's rapid advancement despite his low talent.
He is surprised to become the class monitor after Fang Yuan's rejection.
He becomes the class monitor after Fang Yuan's rejection, a position he initially did not expect.
He is physically assaulted and knocked unconscious by Fang Yuan in front of other students, demonstrating Fang Yuan's continued dominance.
He is humiliated and physically defeated by Fang Yuan, demonstrating the hollowness of his new authority against Fang Yuan's raw power.
Is unsatisfied that Fangzheng surpassed him in cultivation.
Tested as Rank one peak stage.
He struggles to adapt to Fangzheng's close-combat style.
He sustains numerous wounds in his fight against Fangzheng.
He is shocked by Fang Yuan's unimaginable dodging skills.
He intends to train fiercely to avenge his humiliation by Fangzheng tenfold.
Experiences pride in Guyue Mobei's B-grade aptitude, which he uses to subtly provoke Guyue Lian.
Feels proud of his grandson Guyue Mobei's B-grade aptitude.
Considered one of the two most powerful elders and a leader of a major faction in the clan, rivaling Guyue Lian.
Becomes livid when Guyue Lian attempts to claim Fangzheng and actively fights for the right to adopt the A-grade talent.
He views Fang Yuan's first-place achievement as a fluke and a test of character for Mobei, rather than a genuine challenge to the traditional aptitude system.
He is a demanding and strict mentor to Guyue Mobei, seeing Fang Yuan's success as a mere stroke of luck and a test of character for Mobei.
He holds a traditional view on Gu Master aptitude and cultivation progression, dismissing Fang Yuan's achievement as an anomaly rather than a challenge to his beliefs.
Mo Chen suspects Fang Yuan's repeated success is due to 'dog shit luck' rather than skill.
His specific knowledge of the wolf tide's timing and the number of Thunder Crown Wolves.
His belief that Mobei's defeats by Fang Yuan are beneficial for his growth and development.
Guyue Mo Chen possesses deep insight into clan dynamics, the value of 'failure' for growth, and the importance of adhering to clan rules.
Guyue Mo Chen views Mobei's defeats as an opportunity for growth and wants him to overcome Fang Yuan independently.
Guyue Mo Chen is aware of the wolf den situation and the potential for a wolf tide the following year.
Fang Yuan delivered Gao Wan's dismembered body to his family's back door.
Fang Yuan's actions are a brilliant strategic move, combining threat and compromise, and exploiting the Mo family's vulnerabilities regarding reputation and internal politics.
Fang Yuan's C-grade aptitude belies his remarkable wisdom and cunning.
His granddaughter, Mo Yan, is short-sighted and arrogant, needing to learn to prioritize clan interests over personal feelings.
He is furious at Mo Yan for escalating the conflict with Fang Yuan and disrespecting clan rules.
He correctly interprets Fang Yuan's delivery of the dismembered body as a nuanced message combining a threat and an offer of compromise.
He is impressed by Fang Yuan's strategic mind, wisdom, tolerance, and determination, despite his C-grade aptitude.
He punishes Mo Yan and makes a calculated decision regarding Gao Wan's family to protect the clan's reputation.
Fang Yuan believes Guyue Mo Chen will interpret the delivery of Gao Wan's dismembered corpse as both a compromise and a threat, leading the Mo family not to pursue Gao Wan's death.
Chooses a Yellow Camel Longhorn Beetle Gu for endurance in combat.
His acquisition of the Yellow Camel Longhorn Beetle Gu for endurance.
Engages in a real battle with a wild boar during the assessment.
Guyue Mo Chen analyzes the assassin's Gu worms to infer their origin, suggesting it might be an external demonic Gu Master.
Guyue Mo Chen is suspected by Guyue Bo of having secretly recruited Fang Yuan to influence Fangzheng.
Guyue Mo Chen is observing the situation and Fang Yuan's actions, considering whether to recruit him.
His grandson is taking the exam this year.
He believes Fangzheng will be this year's number one.
Is provoked by Guyue Mo Chen's boasting and remains sullen after his own grandson's aptitude is not yet revealed.
Considered one of the two most powerful elders and a leader of a major faction in the clan, rivaling Guyue Mo Chen.
Falsified his grandson Guyue Cheng's aptitude to appear B-grade.
Immediately attempts to claim Fangzheng for his bloodline upon the revelation of his A-grade aptitude, further escalating his rivalry with Mo Chen.
He plans to use a Rank 3 Gu's aura to help Guyue Cheng refine his Moonlight Gu, demonstrating his willingness to use his power to aid his favored grandson.
He is a supportive grandfather, offering guidance and practical help to Guyue Cheng.
He reassures Guyue Cheng, reframing Fang Yuan's success as an insignificant fluke and emphasizing the importance of focusing on true rivals.
He offers to use a Rank 3 Gu's aura to help Guyue Cheng refine his Moonlight Gu, demonstrating a willingness to leverage power for his grandson's benefit.
He is aware of the Cleansing Water Gu's ability to cleanse foreign aura from an aperture and has secured a Liquor worm for Chicheng.
Guyue Lian is performing a dangerous primeval essence infusion to accelerate Chicheng's cultivation, sacrificing his grandson's future potential for the Chi family's immediate gain.
Guyue Lian knows about the Cleansing Water Gu as a potential cure for Chicheng's cultivation side effects and about the rarity of the Liquor worm.
Guyue Lian has used his connections to acquire a Liquor worm for Guyue Chicheng.
He expects his grandson to grow through setbacks.
Guyue Lian advocates for immediate retaliation against the Bai or Xiong clans following the assassination attempt.
Guyue Lian is suspected by Guyue Bo of having secretly recruited Fang Yuan to influence Fangzheng.
He is ashen-faced when his grandson, Chi Cheng, is defeated by Fangzheng.
He hopes his grandson will defeat Fangzheng to bring honor to the Chi clan.
Believed to have B-grade aptitude after taking 36 steps, but Fang Yuan knows it's a falsified C-grade.
Identified as the grandson of Guyue Lian, one of the clan elders.
He is re-motivated by his grandfather's words and assistance, regaining his fighting spirit.
He is initially disappointed by the news of Fang Yuan's success.
He regains his fighting spirit after his grandfather's encouragement and strategic advice.
Guyue Cheng views Guyue Mobei and Fang Yuan as rivals, and is suspicious of Fang Yuan's success.
Guyue Cheng's true talent is C grade, and his B grade appearance is due to cheating.
Guyue Cheng is worried about the new competition focusing purely on cultivation talent, as his B-grade talent is a fabricated illusion.
Guyue Cheng learns he is a pawn in his grandfather's clan power struggles and must maintain a strong image for the Red Branch Family.
Guyue Cheng is aware that his B-grade aptitude is a deception orchestrated by his grandfather.
His understanding that his B-grade aptitude is a deception.
His realization that he is a representation of the Red Branch Family's image in clan politics.
He is surprised and glad to become a vice-class monitor due to Fang Yuan's actions.
He is appointed a vice-class monitor due to Fang Yuan's rejection, seeing it as a step towards greater power.
He is still subjected to Fang Yuan's extortion despite his new position, realizing the limitations of his authority.
He is forced to pay Fang Yuan despite his new position, realizing his new authority is limited.
Chooses a Dragonpill cricket Gu for dodging ability.
His acquisition of the Dragonpill cricket Gu for dodging.
He aims to defeat Fangzheng to 'kill two birds with one stone'.
He relies on his Scarlet Pill Cricket Gu for agility and avoids close combat.
His fighting spirit is severely damaged when Fangzheng points out his weakness.
He has recently advanced to Rank two.
He has recently advanced to Rank two, making him arrogant but still somewhat inexperienced.
Central figure in the 'Hope Gu' legend, whose life story illustrates the function and importance of Strength Gu, Wisdom Gu, and Hope Gu.
Fang Yuan's routine of buying two jars of wine every day for a week.
Recognizes Fang Yuan as a regular customer for wine purchases.
Fought and killed the Flower Wine Monk, but died shortly after from injuries; his story is a central part of Guyue clan lore.
A small-scale beast horde composed of old, weak, sick, or disabled lightning wolves, pushed out by the wolf king due to limited resources.
Humans live in clans for survival against harsh environments, beast tides, and other Gu Masters.
Inhabited by the Guyue clan, whose members do not engage in lowly labor like farming.
Relies on caravans for trade, especially for a year's worth of income.
A stop-over point for merchant caravans.
A regular stop for caravans, serving as a hub for trade and interaction between mortals and Gu Masters.
Historically founded by Rank five experts, known for its resilience and protective leaders.
All property in the village belongs to the Guyue clan.
Mortals are serfs and servants raised by the lord.
Site of the annual Awakening Ceremony where youths discover their cultivation aptitudes.
Center of power for the Guyue village.
Home to the patriarch and site for major discussions and ceremonies.
Front square enshrines ancestor tablets.
The central hub for clan governance and political decision-making.
Original reason for Guyue clan's settlement at Qing Mao Mountain due to its spirit spring.
Spirit spring produces Yuan stone, vital for the clan.
Contains the clan's largest breeding ground for moon orchids, an ingredient for Gu worms.
Part of the Awakening Ceremony's aptitude test; crossing it and progressing through the sea of flowers determines one's talent level.
Part of the Flower Wine Monk's legacy, containing physical challenges.
A part of the Flower Wine Monk's hidden legacy, containing a Gu worm.
A natural formation within Qing Mao Mountain, housing a large beast horde of jade eye stone monkeys.
Part of the Flower Wine Monk's legacy, representing a new challenge.
Generates 'Hope Gu' during the Awakening Ceremony, which coalesce in the bodies of youths and help determine their aptitude.
The place where Gu Master education and initial training takes place for the Guyue clan.
Provides basic education and training for aspiring Gu Masters.
Distributes weekly Yuan stone allowances to students.
Serves as a hub for competition and social hierarchy among young Gu Masters.
Has strict rules protecting students, especially within dormitories.
Gu Masters are forbidden from using Gu to fight within the academy grounds.
The primary institution for nurturing new Gu Masters for the clan.
The place where young Gu Masters are trained, and where clan hierarchy and power struggles manifest among the youth.
Annual year-end exams are held here where Gu Masters observe and recruit students for their groups.
The place where new Gu Masters select their first vital Gu.
Sells Rank one Gu worms and Gu-related supplies.
Grass puppets are a common training tool.
Replenished with Gu worms only once a year.
Requires thousands of Yuan stone daily to feed its Gu worms.
Many desirable Gu worms are often out of stock due to student selection.
A place for commerce and social interaction within the village.
Utilizes a specific type of 'grass Gu' (true mushrooms) for air purification and crystal ladybugs (a type of Gu) for wine storage and dispensing.
The future site of the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance and corpse.
Led by a Rank five Gu Master, known for having powerful Rank four Gu Masters like Jia Fu and Jia Gui competing for leadership.

locations.name: Village at the Foot of the Mountain

nodes.content: Mortal villages at the foot of Qing Mao Mountain are under the rule of the three major Gu Master clans.
These villages provide servants but their inhabitants are not chosen to become Gu Masters, due to the clans' strict bloodline and kinship policies.
The aperture is where Gu Masters store and activate their Gu worms and cultivate primeval essence.
Leads to the location of the Flower Wine Monk's strength inheritance, currently blocked by a massive rock.
Believed by Fang Yuan to contain clues to the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance.
Contains large amounts of permafrost rotten soil.
Responsible for issuing clan missions and approving family property divisions.
Maintains records of family property ownership.
Known for its low rent and day-by-day payment options due to its poor condition.

threads.description: Fang Yuan's desperate attempt to defy death and his use of the Spring Autumn Cicada.
The Guyue Clan's internal politics, power struggles, and competition for talent.
Fang Yuan's internal conflict, strategic planning, and ruthless ambition.
The manipulative and strained relationship between Fang Yuan, his uncle and aunt, and its impact on Fangzheng.
The evolving sibling rivalry and power dynamic between Fang Yuan and Guyue Fangzheng.
The Guyue Clan's struggle with declining bloodline purity and the increasing rarity of high-aptitude individuals.
Fang Yuan's secret goal to leverage his reincarnation memories and knowledge to achieve greater power.
The rivalry and power struggle between Guyue Mo Chen and Guyue Lian, two influential elders.
The complex, antagonistic relationship between Fang Yuan and his younger brother Fangzheng.
Fang Yuan's pursuit of power, resources, and the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance.
The mystery surrounding the Flower Wine Monk's legacy and hidden inheritance.

direct accusation of fratricide, then diverting blame
employer of; pawns in political game

Accusation and foiled attempt at framing
dissatisfaction with lack of proof, asserting clan's dignity
Disgust and frustration due to disrupted plans
Intensified animosity and intent to betray
persuasive, acting on behalf of uncle/aunt
Corrupt alliance based on mutual benefit
Envy and grudging admiration for success
Intense hatred and desire for revenge
pragmatic alliance based on shared secret

nodes.content: Is the legendary demon who killed the fourth generation clan leader, making him a figure of hatred in the Guyue clan.
Is known by Fang Yuan to have left behind a valuable inheritance that will be discovered in the future.
His death and the location of his treasure are central to Fang Yuan's current quest, with conflicting historical accounts.
His true history, including his fight with the Guyue fourth generation leader and his subsequent death, is revealed through the Photo-audio Gu.
His corpse is discovered by Fang Yuan, containing the Liquor worm and some Yuan stones.
Identified as the original owner of the Liquor worm, a Rank five expert whose willpower remains.
His image wall can replay for extended periods, activated by those with 'no good feelings towards the Guyue clan'.
The Flower Wine Monk is revealed to have died in the secret cave, leaving behind his inheritance.
The Flower Wine Monk's inheritance is conditional on the successor destroying the Guyue clan and slaughtering its village.
Left behind an inheritance of strength, altering Fang Yuan's original plans regarding Jia Jin Sheng.
His inheritance, focusing on strength, unexpectedly appeared, disrupting Fang Yuan's original plans.
His inheritance is expected to involve dangerous tests typical of demonic cultivators.
Intentionally blocked the secret cave path with a man-made rock and left an Earth Treasury Flower Gu with a White Boar Gu inside for a future inheritor.
The Flower Wine Monk's legacy is further revealed through a second secret chamber containing a Jade Skin Gu and an underground stone forest with a challenge.
Uncle Guyue reveals his plan to frame Fang Yuan to prevent him from inheriting his parents' property, due to Fang Yuan's rejection of adoption.
Uncle Guyue orchestrates a plan to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him, aiming to secure his parents' inheritance.
Uncle Guyue pretends to be benevolent towards Fangzheng to win him over.
Uncle Guyue is aware of the Guyue family's inheritance rules and uses them to his advantage.
Reveals his detailed plot to undermine Fang Yuan's cultivation and inheritance claim using clan rules and resource control.
Recognizes Fang Yuan's cunning and is surprised by his ability to counter initial plots.
Identifies Fangzheng's resentment as a valuable tool against Fang Yuan.
Plots against Fang Yuan to impede his cultivation and prevent him from inheriting family property.
Knows the clan rules regarding inheritance and cultivation levels.
Uses Fangzheng's resentment towards Fang Yuan as a 'weapon'.
Is surprised by Fang Yuan's cunning and his ability to counter his initial plots.
Provides Fangzheng with 6 Yuan stones for cultivation.
Feigns concern for Fangzheng while subtly directing him against Fang Yuan.
Uncle reveals the two-step process for claiming inheritance: application without mission, then completion of a specific inheritance mission via a small group.
Uncle confirms that Jiao San is a pawn in their scheme to indefinitely block Fang Yuan's inheritance through continuous mission assignments.
Uncle is the mastermind behind the plot to prevent Fang Yuan from claiming his inheritance, using clan rules and Jiao San to create obstacles.
Uncle is aware of the specific clan rules regarding inheritance claims, including the cultivation prerequisite and the mission requirement through small groups.
Aunt Guyue shows her complicity in the scheme to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him, expressing anxiety about the inheritance.
Aunt Guyue is complicit in her husband's plan to disinherit Fang Yuan and frame him.
Aunt Guyue plays the role of a loving guardian to Fangzheng to gain his loyalty.
Understands the full scope of her husband's manipulative plan.
Supports her husband's schemes against Fang Yuan, though she initially misunderstands the depth of his intent.
Her strong desire for Fang Yuan's expulsion and belief that his actions will lead to it.
Her misunderstanding of the academy's rules regarding student fighting.
The Aunt harbors deep resentment towards Fang Yuan, perceiving him as a 'little bastard' and wishing for his expulsion.
The Aunt initially believes Fang Yuan's actions will lead to severe punishment or expulsion, demonstrating a lack of understanding of the clan's martial culture.
Fangzheng's aunt implies their family is in dire financial straits and cannot provide more Yuan stones.
Aunt confirms that the family property records are held by the Internal Affairs Hall, making it difficult to deny Fang Yuan outright.
Aunt is complicit in her husband's schemes to deny Fang Yuan his inheritance, initially panicked but then reassured by his detailed plan.
Aunt is aware that the family property they are withholding from Fang Yuan is 'clearly recorded in the Internal Affairs Hall,' implying a past wrongful acquisition or retention of said property.
Tries to smooth over a tense situation with the waiter and later with the young Gu Master.
Skilled at handling rowdy customers and recognizing when a sale won't happen, later fawns over Gu Masters.
Boasts about the rarity and quality of Green Bamboo Wine.
Believes being a Gu Master is inherently good and powerful, unaware of the costs.
Expresses envy for Gu Masters' status and power.
His knowledge about the upcoming caravan and its impact on the green bamboo wine supply.
He informs Fang Yuan about an upcoming caravan purchase that will deplete the inn's green bamboo wine stock.
Fang Yuan is also a Gu Master, specifically a junior in the academy, due to his primeval essence aura.
Uses his Gu Master status to intimidate and assert dominance over mortals.
Is concerned about lacking 'source stones' (Yuan stones for Gu Masters), indicating personal financial strain despite his status.
Believes mortals are inferior and unworthy of flaunting wealth in front of Gu Masters.
His previous actions are cited as an example of Gu Masters' financial struggles.
His attempt to deceive Fang Yuan about the scarcity and price of grass puppets.
He uses scarcity tactics to encourage students to buy grass puppets quickly, inflating the price for profit.
He pretends there are only a few grass puppets left and then reveals an 'eighth one' to Fang Yuan, implying a special favor, despite the actual cost being much lower.
Advises Jiang Ya to show restraint and not make things difficult for mortals.
Is successfully captured by Fang Yuan after becoming intoxicated.
Is revealed to have betrayed and ambushed the Flower Wine Monk, leading to both their deaths, a historical truth suppressed by the Guyue clan.
Witnessed the battle between the Flower Wine Monk and the Fourth Generation Tribe Leader, and later engaged in battle with the Monk.
Contains the remains of the Flower Wine Monk.
Home to a Photo-audio Gu that records past events.
This cave was likely created by the Thousand Miles Earthwolf Spider, not naturally formed.
It contains the image wall and the inheritance of the Flower Wine Monk.
Contains the inheritance of the Flower Wine Monk.
The soil inside the tunnel is strangely crimson and emits a dim light.
A hidden passage deliberately obstructed by the Flower Wine Monk.
Serves as a clandestine storage for Fang Yuan's collected items and a gateway to the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance.

threads.description: The social hierarchy and power dynamics among Gu Masters and mortals.
The political tension and historical cover-up surrounding the Guyue clan's past and the Flower Wine Monk.
Fang Yuan's ongoing struggle for resources and power.

nodes.content: It is a Rank 6 Gu, Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, severely weakened from reversing time but still highly potent.
Its presence as Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, despite his low rank, is an unprecedented anomaly.
It is revealed to be the mechanism of Fang Yuan's rebirth, having reversed 500 years of time at great cost to its vitality.
It is a Rank 6 Gu, Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, currently in a severely weakened and dormant state but capable of emitting an intimidating aura.
Its presence as Fang Yuan's lifebound Gu, despite his Rank 1 status and the Cicada's Rank 6, is an unprecedented anomaly.
They mistake Fangzheng for Fang Yuan due to their similar appearance.
Fang Yuan killed Gao Wan without emotion.
Fang Yuan's power as a Gu Master is terrifying.
The academy guards are bound by clan rules to protect students within the academy, especially Fang Yuan.
The guards witness Fang Yuan's brutal killing of Gao Wan and are struck with fear and terror, recognizing the power of a Gu Master.
The guards are compelled to obey Fang Yuan's commands, even after he commits murder, due to his status as a Gu Master and their fear.
The guards witness Fang Yuan's use of middle stage primeval essence via the moonblade, which could expose his secret if they understand its implications.
His role in actively spreading negative rumors about Fang Yuan.
Spread negative rumors and criticisms about Fang Yuan's actions during the beast horde.
A regular source of green bamboo wine, a key ingredient for feeding certain Gu worms.
Caravans periodically visit to purchase local goods like green bamboo wine.
The area is specifically designed for Gu Master training.
The bamboo walls sometimes grow wild roses.

threads.description: Fang Yuan's desperate acquisition of resources to cultivate and refine Gu, a constant struggle against his C-grade aptitude and limited funds.
The mystery surrounding the Liquor worm and its true potential or danger.
The competition and rivalry among the new generation of Gu Masters.
The clan elders' influence and manipulation of the younger generation.
The system of Gu Master cultivation, its requirements, and societal structure.

nodes.content: His demotion by the Academy Elder for disrespect.
The Guard Leader is stripped of his position by the Academy Elder for questioning his judgment and disrespecting a clan member.
His plan to sow discord between Fangzheng and Fang Yuan, using the latter's actions to secure Fangzheng's loyalty.
Guyue Dong Tu plans to sow discord between Fangzheng and Fang Yuan, using Fang Yuan's actions to distance Fangzheng from him and make Fangzheng useful to their household.
Guyue Dong Tu understands the academy's hidden rules regarding student fighting and why Fang Yuan won't be punished.
Fang Yuan has advanced to Rank two and applied for a family splitting mission.
The male Gu Master is susceptible to bribery and can be used to obstruct Fang Yuan.
He feigns politeness to the internal affairs hall Gu Master while planning to use clan rules against Fang Yuan.
He fears Fang Yuan's rapid cultivation speed and potential to acquire inheritance.
He knows that hiring outsiders violates clan rules, providing leverage against Fang Yuan.
His observation of increased student passion for martial arts and his misunderstanding of its source.
The Martial Arts Instructor observes a dramatic increase in student passion and dedication to basic martial arts, unaware of Fang Yuan's role as the catalyst.
He is aware that Fang Yuan is close to exhaustion and that aggressive students could defeat him.
He presides over the academy matches and attempts to motivate students.
He is frustrated by the students' fear of Fang Yuan and their unwillingness to fight.
He clashes verbally with Fang Yuan over his aggressive fighting style and defiance.
He attempts to use the prize and his authority to force Fang Yuan to continue fighting or lose face, but fails.
Her awareness of Mobei's injuries and suspicion about their cause, as well as her recent mission.
Guyue Mo Yan is fiercely protective of her younger brother, Mobei, and seeks to avenge his humiliation.
Guyue Mo Yan has recently completed a scouting mission concerning a wolf den and its potential for a wolf tide.
Guyue Mo Yan is initially fooled by Fang Yuan's similar appearance to Fangzheng, leading to hesitation before confronting him.
Despite her grandfather's advice, Guyue Mo Yan intends to intervene in Mobei's conflict with Fang Yuan in her own way.
Her intention to act against Fang Yuan to protect her brother, despite her grandfather's advice.
Her initial hesitation and misidentification of Fang Yuan as Fangzheng.
Her realization that she has been tricked by Fang Yuan's resemblance to Fangzheng and his intentional deception.
Fang Yuan is adept at using the family rules to his advantage.
Fang Yuan is a 'wolf cub' and 'cunning'.
Mo Yan is outsmarted by Fang Yuan's exploitation of family rules and her own psychological weaknesses, failing to capture or punish him.
Mo Yan is forced to leave Fang Yuan's dormitory due to family rules and her inability to provoke him, leaving Gao Wan to guard.
Fang Yuan sent Gao Wan's dismembered body to the Mo family's back door.
Her grandfather, Guyue Mo Chen, is extremely angry at her for violating clan rules by escalating the conflict with Fang Yuan.
Fang Yuan's act of sending the body is a complex strategic message, not just a simple provocation.
Fang Yuan possesses exceptional wisdom, tolerance, and determination despite his C-grade aptitude.
She is enraged and humiliated by Fang Yuan's actions and the perceived disrespect to her family by killing Gao Wan.
She initially misinterprets Fang Yuan's delivery of the body as pure provocation.
She is confined for seven days by her grandfather for her role in instigating the conflict and failing to recognize Fang Yuan's strategic depth.
She resolves to kill Gao Wan's entire family to uphold the Mo family's reputation, showing her ruthlessness.
Mo Yan previously caused trouble for Fang Yuan, which Fang Yuan now interprets as 'breaking the rules'.
Her past regret of only being a vice class monitor motivates her support for Mobei's ambition.
Guyue Mo Yan was worried about Guyue Mobei's injury and potential emotional trauma.
Guyue Mo Yan encourages Mobei's fighting spirit and ambition, reminding him he is a future leader.
Guyue Mo Yan expresses a past regret about only being a vice class monitor, hoping Mobei can achieve the monitor position.
She is worried about Mo Bei's performance and wishes to help him against Fangzheng.
She views Fang Yuan as a 'battle maniac' similar to Chi Shan.
His misidentification of Fang Yuan and his eagerness to please Mo Yan.
Gao Wan mistakenly identifies Fang Yuan as Fangzheng due to their similar appearance, causing him significant embarrassment.
Gao Wan is a loyal servant of the Mo family, eager to prove himself and earn his master's favor.
His further embarrassment at having misidentified Fang Yuan.
Fang Yuan has offended the Mo faction.
Gao Wan is ordered by Mo Yan to guard Fang Yuan's dormitory.
Gao Wan is a skilled mortal martial artist, trained in fists and legs, capable of posing a significant threat to an initial stage Gu Master.
Gao Wan initially underestimates Fang Yuan's cunning and fighting ability, leading to his severe injury and eventual death.
Gao Wan is unaware of Fang Yuan's refined middle stage primeval essence, which makes his moonblade far more lethal than expected.
Fang Yuan is a 'wolf cub' whose attacks are 'ruthless and sneaky'.
Fang Yuan's moonblade is unusually powerful, capable of cutting bone.
Gao Wan was killed by Fang Yuan, and his death is used by Fang Yuan as part of a strategic message to the Mo family.
He was previously killed by Fang Yuan, contributing to Fang Yuan's fearsome reputation.
Students are provided with basic living arrangements.
The place where basic combat techniques and Gu usage are taught.
Equipped with various training puppets for students to practice on.

The only way to leave the academy for students.

nodes.content: The Old Man recounts the legend of Ren Zu and the Hope Gu to his grandson.

Identified by Guyue Bo as an 'unpolished gem' to be trained by Fang Yuan's challenges.
Jia Fu is revealed to be a Rank four Gu Master and the leader of the caravan.
Jia Fu explains the caravan's delay, citing encounters with dark blood bats and the mountain fog on Precipice Mountain.
Is a Rank four Gu Master and the leader of the caravan, arriving to settle a dispute.
Is the older brother of Jia Jin Sheng, whose name is used to intimidate others.
Jia Fu uses his authority and wealth to publicly resolve his brother's scam, portraying himself as righteous while likely using the situation to further suppress Jia Jinsheng.
He was tasked by the Jia Family Head to lead the caravan with Jia Jin Sheng, partly as a test of his temperament and ability to tolerate his brother.
He deliberately arranged for Jia Jin Sheng and Jia Fu to travel together, hoping Jia Jin Sheng would learn reality and live a peaceful life, and to test Jia Fu's character.
Is being tested by the Jia family head through his leadership of the caravan alongside Jia Jin Sheng.
He has returned to Guyue village.
His actions (standing) are intended to project power and determination to both the Guyue clan and his own father.
Is anxious about his missing brother, Jia Jin Sheng, and feels responsible for him.
Uses his Rank four Gu Master status to exert pressure on the Guyue clan.
Is trying to appear determined to his father and the Guyue clan regarding his brother's disappearance.
Realizes he is a target in the internal Jia clan power struggle, specifically from Jia Gui.
Knows the capabilities and limitations of the Jia clan's tracking Gu, the Hell Path Butterfly.
Fang Yuan was involved in a transaction with Jia Jin Sheng at a gambling den.
Jia Jin Sheng pursued Fang Yuan over the Liquor worm and threatened him, which aligns with Jia Jin Sheng's character.
His rival, Jia Gui, is now a primary suspect in Jia Jin Sheng's murder, and this makes him vulnerable.
The Guyue Clan leader will protect Fang Yuan, even at political cost.
Fang Yuan is innocent of Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance.
Believes Jia Gui is the mastermind behind Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance but lacks evidence.
Understands that framing Fang Yuan could serve as an 'explanation' for Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance, deflecting blame from himself.
Fails to grasp the Guyue Clan's strategic interest in protecting Fang Yuan, leading to frustration.
Resolves to thoroughly investigate Jia Jin Sheng's disappearance by hiring 'God of Arrest Tie Xue Leng'.
He believes Jia Gui is responsible for Jia Jin Sheng's death and plans to frame him.
He harbors intense anger and a desire for revenge against Jia Gui.
He plans to use his apparent diligence in investigating Jia Jin Sheng's death to deflect blame from himself and frame Jia Gui, ensuring his father believes his 'attitude'.
He is intensely angry at Jia Gui, believing him to be the true culprit behind Jia Jin Sheng's death and vows revenge.
Home to the Guyue village and various beast nests.
Protected by strict family rules, no one is allowed to break in and capture students.

Her growing surprise and changing perception of Fang Yuan.

Accused of selling a fake Black Boar Gu for an exorbitant price, denying the accusation.

Fang Yuan knows about the secret Jia clan competition for family assets, which is meant to be confidential.

Fang Yuan chose him for this transaction because of his weaker cultivation and manipulability, not because of his 'honesty'.

The Flower Wine Monk's inheritance is contingent on destroying the Guyue clan and slaughtering its village.
He plans to kill Fang Yuan to claim the Flower Wine Monk's inheritance entirely for himself.
He was murdered by Fang Yuan the previous night, his body disposed of and the scene covered by the heavy rain.

Killed by Fang Yuan the previous night, his body disposed of to conceal the crime.
Fang Yuan initially saw him as a chess piece for disposing of stolen goods, inciting conflict, and infiltrating the Jia family.

Is considered a burden by his father to Jia Fu.
His disappearance is implied to be part of a larger internal Jia clan power struggle.

Their collective support for Fang Yuan against Jia Jin Sheng's aggressive tactics.

nodes.content: He observes Jia Fu's deep-seated suspicion and anger towards Jia Gui.

Cooperates with Fang Yuan out of fear for his life, including slandering Old Man Wang.
Fang Yuan is capable of executing people without hesitation, even after they've complied.

Fang Yuan is a powerful and ruthless Gu Master who killed Wang Er and is threatening his family.

nodes.content: Fang Yuan killed her brother, Wang Er, and is threatening her family.

Is revealed to have been hiding the truth about his son, Wang Da, and his activities.
Drew a map for Fang Yuan but omitted critical information to protect his son.

Is a Rank two Gu Master stationed in the village by the Guyue clan.

Is aware of Fang Yuan's reputation for ruthlessness and his recent acquisition of valuable Gu.
Fang Yuan was responsible for the deaths of Wang Da and the hunters, and that concealing this protects his own career.

Is revealed to have faked his death three years ago and become a demonic Gu Master.
Is now seeking revenge on Fang Yuan for killing his family.
There are tens of Rank 2 Gu Master invigilators and a few Rank 3 clan elders guarding the assessment area.
Guyue Sou is an experienced Rank 2 Gu Master, and there is another middle-aged Gu Master specializing in close combat.
He killed his wife, Wan Er, to refine the Love Separation Gu.
Wang Da's primary motivation is an all-consuming hatred for Fang Yuan, stemming from the death of his family.
Wang Da is a cunning and ruthless Rank 2 demonic Gu Master, tempered by three years of precarious survival, possessing Love Separation and Phantom Tracking Gu.
Wang Da plans to eliminate Gu Master invigilators before attacking his target, demonstrating tactical thinking.
Wang Da justifies his brutal actions, including killing his wife, as necessary for survival and his 'demonic path'.
Wang Da mistakenly believes Fangzheng is Fang Yuan, leading to his demise.
Wang Da's death is confirmed, officially ruled a suicide, but implicitly understood to be related to Fang Yuan's actions.
Was murdered by Old Man Wang's family to prevent her from revealing Wang Da's secret.
Usually dry, but recently flooded due to heavy rains.
A miniature waterfall has carved a crack into a large boulder, leading to a secret cave.
Home of Old Man Wang's family, implying a life of solitude away from the main village.
Students are rewarded with Yuan stones for advancement.
Mid-year and end-of-year assessments are held to temper students.
One of Wang Da's secret residences.
Contains clues to Wang Da's faked death and revenge plot.

The mystery surrounding the 'butterfly effect' and Fang Yuan's impact on future events.
Wang Da's quest for revenge against Fang Yuan.

nodes.content: He uses a Flash Gu and hair-based armor/spikes in combat.
Guyue Sou is an experienced Rank 2 Gu Master, capable of using Flash Gu and defensive hair armor, showcasing his combat prowess.
He uses Rock Skin Gu and Jade Wind Wheel in combat.
A close-combat focused Rank 2 Gu Master, skilled in using Rock Skin Gu and Jade Wind Wheel.
Guyue Yao Ji is the clan's top healing Gu Master, responsible for Fangzheng's recovery.
The Punishment Hall Elder is responsible for investigating the assassin's identity and methods.
The Dark Hall Elder accepts Guyue Bo's order to plan a response to the external threat targeting Fangzheng.

A Rank two upper stage Gu Master, representing the Chi faction. Was once considered an A-grade talent but tested as B-grade, leading to a calmer temperament. He is one of the most elite Rank two Gu Masters in the clan.
A Rank two upper stage Gu Master, representing the Chi faction.
Was once considered an A-grade talent but tested as B-grade, leading to a calmer temperament. He is one of the most elite Rank two Gu Masters in the clan.
Tested as Rank one middle stage.
She is evenly matched with Mobei for a time but loses due to stamina.
Tested as Rank one peak stage, has B-grade talent and is Guyue Lian's grandson.

threads.description: The ongoing rivalry and political tension between the Guyue, Bai, and Xiong clans.

nodes.content: Shen Mama is the most valued servant of Aunt Guyue and manages the household.
Mother Shen is sent by Fang Yuan's uncle and aunt to persuade him to return home and prevent him from renting a house independently.
The clan leader instructed not to recruit Fang Yuan due to his disharmonious relationship with Fangzheng and a desire for observation.
Fangzheng and Fang Yuan are twin brothers, with Fangzheng being an A-grade talent and Fang Yuan a C-grade talent.
Fang Yuan is a 'battle maniac' who doesn't care about life and death in combat.
Fangzheng is a good seedling, talented and dependable, whose growth could alleviate the burden of fighting Bai Ning Bing.
Annual year-end exams are held here where Gu Masters observe and recruit students for their groups.
The clan leader instructed not to recruit Fang Yuan due to his disharmonious relationship with Fangzheng and a desire for observation.
Fang Yuan has 'guts' and is 'lawless,' even bullying his younger brother.
Fangzheng's cultivation to Rank two in the academy with solid foundational skills is rare, and he is highly valued by the clan leader.
He is an arch-enemy of Mo Yan's faction, and observes the battles intently.
His eyes flicker with interest during Fang Yuan's daring combat style.
He is an effective leader, making quick decisions and showing some tolerance and strategic thinking.
He notes Fang Yuan's unexpected physical endurance and stamina.
Guyue Jiao San exists and is a low-status Gu Master who unexpectedly invites Fang Yuan to his team.
Guyue Jiao San unexpectedly invites Fang Yuan to join his team, disrupting the clan elders' plans.
Guyue Jiao San's warm attitude is a mask, indicating he has ulterior motives for inviting Fang Yuan.
Guyue Jiao San is actively observing Fang Yuan and maintains a deceptively warm demeanor.
Jiao San's authority and control over the team are implicitly challenged by Fang Yuan's unexpected competence.
Jiao San is actively working with Fang Yuan's uncle and aunt to prevent Fang Yuan from claiming his inheritance by continuously assigning him to missions and controlling his evaluation.
Jiao San feigns care and mentorship towards Fang Yuan while subtly trying to suppress him and control his access to resources and opportunities.
Jiao San is acting as a tool for Fang Yuan's uncle and aunt, attempting to manipulate Fang Yuan through team dynamics and mission assignments.
His suspicion that Fang Yuan might be attempting to break through to Rank Two independently.
Was completely misled by Fang Yuan's deception, believing he was attempting to cultivate in the rented room or had encountered wild beasts.
His anger and embarrassment over Fang Yuan's absence reveal his desire to assert dominance over new group members.
Immediately interpreted Fang Yuan's note as an attempt to breakthrough and devised a plan to interrupt him.
His belief that Fang Yuan's note is an application for leave and a deliberate attempt to breakthrough Rank Two.
His misinterpretation of Fang Yuan's activities, believing Fang Yuan is delayed by coal excavation or has encountered wild beasts.
His recognition that the Landlord is a Gu Master and has connections, requiring him to control his temper.
He becomes convinced of Fang Yuan's incompetence and stupidity, believing Fang Yuan is responsible for Hua Xin's death and the King Boar's escape.
He wants to injure Fang Yuan and delay his growth, acting on a promise to Guyue Dong Tu.
He abandons his remaining group members to save himself from the lightning wolves, likely leading to his death.
Was suffocated by Fang Yuan under the guise of grief.
Kong Jing possesses a Rank Two Big Belly Frog Gu used for logistics, capable of storing items and spitting out tools.
Kong Jing actively supports Jiao San's efforts to manipulate Fang Yuan, including a 'suggestion' for Fang Yuan to rent a cold, unhealthy basement.
His disdain for Fang Yuan as 'just a young newbie' despite his year-end exam performance.
Follows Jiao San's orders and attempts to aid in his schemes, even suggesting actions to further their plan.
He blames Fang Yuan for Hua Xin's death and expresses intense frustration at Fang Yuan's feigned incompetence.
He follows Jiao San in a panicked retreat, likely leading to his death.
The primary venue for the year-end exam combat trials.
